<title level="1" pfs="">[Ki Adept](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1451)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The ki adept is in tune with their own body and can manipulate the mystical energy that flows within them. Typically, the ki adept works to hamper as many foes as possible with their Knocking Sweep and follows up with a Flurry of Blows.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Ki Adept](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1451)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 24

**Perception** +23

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +23

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=17)_, _[bracers of armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=416)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280), [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [dart](/Weapons.aspx?ID=68)_  (3), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 33 

**Fort** +23 

**Ref** +23 

**Will** +23 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 230

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_bo staff_ +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=190), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)),
**Damage** 2d8+11 bludgeoning plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_fist_ +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)),
**Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_dart_ +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)),
**Damage** 2d6+9 bludgeoning

**Monk Focus Spells** DC 32, attack +25, (3 Focus Points)
- **7th**
[Abundant Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=482), [Ki Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=484), [Ki Rush](/Spells.aspx?ID=485), [Ki Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=486), [Wholeness of Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=488), [Wind Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=490)

**Flurry of Blows** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) The ki adept makes two Strikes with their fist or with a [monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=112) weapon. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

**Knocking Sweep** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) The ki adept swings their weapon with a sweeping spin. They attempt separate [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) any number of creatures within their reach. Each attempt counts toward the ki adept's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after they makes all the attacks.

**Powerful Fists**   The ki adept's fist Strikes are treated as [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) and [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) and don't take penalties when making lethal attacks.

**Punch Down**   The ki adept deals an extra 2d10 damage to [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) creatures.

</column>
name: Ki Adept

<title level="1" pfs="">[Archery Specialist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1452)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Archery specialists weaken their foes from a great distance. They typically begin combat by attempting to pin the most dangerous target, then follow up with a Rain of Arrows on a clumped group.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Archery Specialist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1452)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 24

**Perception** +26

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +24, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +5

**Con** +4

**Int** +3

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74)_, _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 35 

**Fort** +22 

**Ref** +26 

**Will** +19 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 190

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)),
**Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_composite longbow_ +27 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)),
**Damage** 3d8+7 piercing

**Pinning Shot** <actions string="Single Action" />  The archery specialist makes a longbow Strike. If the attack hits, it deals no damage and instead imparts the [bow critical specialization effect](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3). If the attack is a critical hit, the target must succeed at two separate Interact actions to pull the missile free.

**Point-Blank Shot**   The archery specialist ignores the penalty to their attack rolls from the [volley](/Traits.aspx?ID=201) trait.

**Rain of Arrows** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) The archery specialist fires several arrows at once, each aimed to land in a 10-foot burst within 60 feet. The archery specialist makes a longbow Strike and compares the attack roll result to the AC of all creatures in the burst. The specialist rolls damage only once and applies it to each creature hit. Rain of Arrows counts as three attacks for the archery specialist's multiple attack penalty.

</column>
name: Archery Specialist

<title level="1" pfs="">[Mage of Many Styles](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1453)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The mage of many styles combines magical and martial tactics. They try to hamper foes with [_black tentacles_](/Spells.aspx?ID=23) or [_confusion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), switching to damaging spells like [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=172) once their opponents are properly lined up. When engaged in melee, they make liberal use of [_shocking grasp_](/Spells.aspx?ID=283) and [_true strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) to deal powerful blows.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 33

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Mage of Many Styles](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1453)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 25

**Perception** +22

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43)

**Skills**
[Academia Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +26, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +28, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +26

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +5

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Items**
_[bracers of armor I](/Equipment.aspx?ID=416)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [handwraps of mighty blows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 32 

**Fort** +22 

**Ref** +23 

**Will** +21 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 220

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)),
**Damage** 2d6+5 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_longsword_ +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)),
**Damage** 2d8+5 slashing

**Wizard Prepared Spells** DC 32, attack +26
- **Cantrips (7th)**
[Acid Splash](/Spells.aspx?ID=3), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330)
- **1st**
[Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=122), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345)
- **2nd**
[Acid Arrow](/Spells.aspx?ID=2), [Mirror Image](/Spells.aspx?ID=197), [Touch of Idiocy](/Spells.aspx?ID=341)
- **3rd**
[Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354)
- **4th**
[Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125)
- **5th**
[Black Tentacles](/Spells.aspx?ID=23), [Shocking Grasp](/Spells.aspx?ID=283), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345)
- **6th**
[Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=33), [Disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=76), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=353)
- **7th**
[Shocking Grasp](/Spells.aspx?ID=283), [True Target](/Spells.aspx?ID=346)

**Wizard School Spells** 2 Focus Points
- **7th**
[Hand of the Apprentice](/Spells.aspx?ID=530), [Physical Boost](/Spells.aspx?ID=533)

**Spell Punch** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) **Trigger** The mage of many styles begins to Cast a Spell that targets at least 1 creature; **Effect** The mage channels the spell through their fist. The mage makes a fist Strike against a target within reach. If the Strike is successful, the target is subjected to the spell. If the spell required an attack roll, the target is hit automatically upon a successful Strike. If the spell required a saving throw, the target takes a –2 status penalty to its saving throw. If the spell could target multiple creatures, it targets only the creature the mage hit with their fist Strike.

</column>
name: Mage of Many Styles

<title level="1" pfs="">[Ghost Monk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1454)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 26

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 9">[Ghost Monk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1454)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Ghost" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=217" />
<trait label="Incorporeal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=222" />
<trait label="Spirit" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=149" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 15

**Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43)

**Skills**
[Irori Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +17, [Temple Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -5

**Dex** +4

**Con** +0

**Int** +2

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Site Bound** A typical ghost can stray only a short distance from where it was killed or the place it haunts. A typical limit is 120 feet. Some ghosts are instead bound to a room, building, item, or creature that was special to it rather than a location.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +19 

**Will** +21 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 115
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation)

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When a ghost is destroyed, it re-forms after 2d4 days within the location it's bound to, fully healed. A ghost can be permanently destroyed only if someone determines the reason for its existence and sets right whatever prevents the spirit from resting. Putting the abbot in A8 to rest brings peace to the ghost monks, allowing them to move on to the Serene Circle in [Axis](/Planes.aspx?ID=14).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ghostly fist +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d8+12 negative

**Flurry of Blows** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) The ghost monk makes two Strikes with their ghostly fist. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

**Frightful Moan** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The ghost laments its fate, forcing each living creature within 30 feet to attempt a DC 25 Will save. On a failure, a creature becomes [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (or frightened 3 on a critical failure). On a success, a creature is temporarily immune to this ghost's frightful moan for 1 minute.

**Telekinetic Assault** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The ghost cries out in pain and anguish as small objects and debris fly about in a 30-foot emanation. Creatures in this area take 5d6 bludgeoning damage, subject to a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.

</column>
name: Ghost Monk
resistance_markdown: all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="LN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />

**Site Bound** A typical ghost can stray only a short distance from where it was killed or the place it haunts. A typical limit is 120 feet. Some ghosts are instead bound to a room, building, item, or creature that was special to it rather than a location.

([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation)

[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When a ghost is destroyed, it re-forms after 2d4 days within the location it's bound to, fully healed. A ghost can be permanently destroyed only if someone determines the reason for its existence and sets right whatever prevents the spirit from resting. Burying Kannitri's remains in the courtyard or carving her name into the pagoda sculpture there (area A3) permanently puts her spirit to rest.

</column>

**Draining Touch** <actions string="Two Actions" />  With a touch, the ghost attempts to drain a living creature's life force. It makes a ghostly hand Strike but deals no damage on a hit. Instead, the target becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) for 1 day, and the ghost regains HP equal to half its own level.

**Flurry of Blows** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73)) Kannitri makes two Strikes with her ghostly fist. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

The creature being landed upon can attempt a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. **Pin Prey** **Trigger** The tyrannosaurus critically hits a Large or smaller foe with its **foot**. **Effect** The creature struck by the foot is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) and the tyrannosaurus uses its foot to hold the creature in place. As long as the tyrannosaurus doesn't move from its position, the pinned creature is [Grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20). A tyrannosaurus gains a +2 circumstance bonus to attack a creature it has pinned in this manner, but it cannot use swallow whole on the target unless it uses its jaws to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the victim first. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Medium, 4d6+8 bludgeoning, Rupture 34 **Tail Swipe** The tyrannosaurus sweeps its tail in a 15-foot cone, dealing 4d8+12 bludgeoning damage to all creatures in the cone. Creatures in the area must attempt a DC 34 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is pushed back 10 feet, and falls prone. **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** Huge or smaller, foot, DC 34

**Rejuvenation** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) When the watchtower poltergeist is destroyed, it re-forms, fully healed, at the watchtower after 12 hours. Destroying the poltergeist and lighting its watchtower's brazier permanently destroys it.
**Telekinetic Defense** **Trigger** A creature approaches within 10 feet of the poltergeist; **Effect** The poltergeist makes a telekinetic object Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Speed** fly 20 feet **Ranged** telekinetic object +27 ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d12+10 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing (depending on object) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 35, attack +25 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177) - **7th** [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333) (at will) **Frighten** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Requirement** The poltergeist must be [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26). **Effect** The poltergeist becomes visible, appearing as a skeletal, ghostlike humanoid. Each creature within 30 feet must attempt a DC 33 Will save, becoming [frightened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) on a failure. On a critical failure, it's also [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) for as long as it's frightened. On a success, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 minute. At the start of its next turn, the poltergeist becomes invisible again. **Telekinetic Storm** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The poltergeist telekinetically throws numerous small objects, such as dozens of pieces of silverware or books, either spreading them out among multiple foes or directing them at one target.
  • When this effect is spread out among multiple foes, the poltergeist makes a telekinetic object Strike at a –2 penalty against each creature within 30 feet. These count as one attack for the poltergeist's multiple attack penalty, and the penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks.
  • When this effect has only one target, the poltergeist makes a telekinetic object Strike against the target, and the damage increases to 4d12+15. It deals 2d12 damage on a failure, and no damage on a critical failure.
name: Watchtower Poltergeist

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Watchtower Shadow](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1462) **Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 36 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +33 **Str** -5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +0 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Site Bound** The shadow is tied to its watchtower. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +29 **Will** +26 **HP** 190 (rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all 15 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance against non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Weaknesses** light vulnerability **Light Vulnerability** An object shedding magical light (such as from the [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) spell) is treated as [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) when used to attack the shadow.
**Rejuvenation** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) When the watchtower shadow is destroyed, it re-forms, fully healed, at the watchtower after 12 hours. Destroying the shadow and lighting its watchtower's brazier permanently destroys it. --- **Speed** fly 30 feet **Melee** shadow hand +30 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 4d10+10 negative **Divine Innate Spells** DC 36 - **5th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) (at will) **Shadow Spawn** When a creature's shadow is pulled free by Steal Shadow, it becomes a shadow spawn under the command of the shadow that created it. This shadow spawn doesn't have Steal Shadow and is perpetually and incurably [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3). If the creature the shadow spawn was pulled from dies, the shadow spawn becomes a full-fledged, autonomous shadow. If the creature recovers from its enfeeblement, its shadow returns to it and the shadow spawn is extinguished. **Slink in Shadows** The shadow can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) or end its [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63) in a creature's or object's shadow. **Steal Shadow** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirement** The shadow hit a living creature with a shadow hand Strike on its previous action. **Effect** The shadow pulls at the target's shadow, making the creature [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13). This is cumulative with other enfeebled conditions from shadows, to a maximum of enfeebled 4. If this increases a creature's enfeebled value to 3 or more, the target's shadow is separated from its body (see shadow spawn). Enfeebled from Steal Shadow decreases by 1 every hour.

**Site Bound** The wraith is tied to its watchtower. --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +28 **Will** +28 +1 status to all saves vs. [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128) **HP** 285 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all 15 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**Sunlight Powerlessness** A wraith caught in sunlight is [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) and [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3).
**Rejuvenation** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) When the watchtower wraith is destroyed, it re-forms, fully healed, at the watchtower after 12 hours. Destroying the wraith and lighting its watchtower's brazier permanently destroys it. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Melee** spectral hand +32 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d8+14 negative plus drain life **Drain Life** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the wraith damages a living creature with its spectral hand Strike, the wraith gains 15 temporary Hit Points and the creature must succeed at a DC 36 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). Further damage dealt by the wraith increases the amount of drain by 1 on a failed save to a maximum of drained 4. **Wraith Spawn** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) A living humanoid slain by a wraith's spectral hand Strike rises as a wraith spawn after 1d4 rounds. This wraith spawn is under the command of the wraith that killed it. It doesn't have drain life or wraith spawn and becomes [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for as long as it is a wraith spawn. If the creator of the wraith spawn dies, the wraith spawn becomes a full-fledged, autonomous wraith; it regains its free will, gains Wraith Spawn, and is no longer clumsy.

The cauthooj Strides up to half its Speed, and this movement does not trigger reactions. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** beak +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+15 piercing **Staccato Strike** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) With subtle alterations in the pitch and tone of its song, the cauthooj directs one creature [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) by its Warbling Song to make a Strike. This works like other Strikes made by confused creatures, except that the cauthooj chooses the target. If no target is in reach or range, or the creature is unable to Strike for any other reason, this ability has no effect. **Warbling Song** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The cauthooj gives a strange, ululating cry that causes nearby creatures to lash out violently and without control. Each creature within 120-foot emanation that can hear the cauthooj must attempt a DC 34 Will save to resist the effect.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is confused for 1 round and immediately attacks itself (in the normal fashion for attacking oneself while confused). This Strike doesn't give the creature a flat check to recover from the confusion.
name: Elder Cauthooj

When the team is reduced to 8 or fewer squares, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Run Them Over!** Hana's Hundreds attempt to trample all foes in their way. The team Forms Up and Strides twice, moving through the space of Medium or smaller creatures. Each creature the team moves through takes 4d8+14 bludgeoning damage (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a critical failure, the creature is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). **Troop Movement** Whenever Hana's Hundreds Strides, the team first Forms Up as a free action to condense into a 20-foot-by-20-foot area (minus any missing squares), then moves up to its Speed. This works just like a Gargantuan creature moving; for instance, if any square of the team enters difficult terrain, the extra movement cost applies to the whole team. **Whirlwind of Blades** **Frequency** once per round; Hana's Hundreds flail their swords wildly at each enemy adjacent to the team (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The damage depends on the number of actions.

**Damage** 2d4+15 slashing

**Butterfly's Kiss**   The warrior can make [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) Strikes with a butterfly sword without taking a penalty.

**Mastermind's Eye**   When the warrior successfully identifies a creature using [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), that creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against their attacks until the start of its next turn; if they critically succeed, it's flat-footed against their attacks for 1 minute.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   The warrior's Strikes deal an additional 1d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

**Studied Strike** <actions string="Single Action" />  The warrior makes a telegraphed attack to learn about their foe. They make a melee Strike. On a hit, they can immediately attempt a check to Recall Knowledge about the target. On a critical hit, they gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the check to Recall Knowledge. The target is then temporarily immune to Studied Strike for 1 day

</column>

**Empowered Hair**   Tak-nwa has mastered using her prehensile hair in combat. She can use her living hair to perform precise tasks such as picking up objects and performing simple Interact actions, and she can automatically [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) with her hair, as the creature ability. She can also deliver hexes through her hair. Whenever Tak-nwa successfully casts a hex focus spell that requires 2 or more actions to cast and that doesn't require a spell attack roll, if her target is within reach, as part of the spellcasting activity she can make a hair Strike against the foe before applying any effects of the hex. If this Strike misses, the hex has no effect.

**Familiar**   Tak-nwa's familiar is an elephant hawk moth named Yon-so. Yon-so has a fly Speed of 40 feet, [darkvision](/Familiars.aspx?ID=5), and [scent](/Familiars.aspx?ID=11) as an imprecise sense to a range of 30 feet.

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Shino Hakusa](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1475) **Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 63 **Perception** +22 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43), [Ysoki](/Languages.aspx?ID=61) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +28, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +27, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +24, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +26, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +26, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +26 **Str** +5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Items** +2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) _handwraps of mighty blows_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [shurikens](/Weapons.aspx?ID=79)_ (5) --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +28 **Will** +22 **HP** 250 **Nimble Dodge** **Trigger** Hakusa is targeted with a melee or ranged attack by an attacker she can see; **Effect** Hakusa gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** _fist_ +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199)), **Damage** 3d6+11 bludgeoning **Ranged** _shuriken_ +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d4+11 piercing **Disorienting Tumble** Hakusa leaps around and over her opponents, disorienting them, to gain the upper hand in combat. When she successfully [Tumbles Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29) a creature's space, that creature becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the end of Hakusa's turn. **Powerful Fists** Hakusa's fist Strikes are treated as [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) and [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379). She doesn't take penalties when making lethal attacks. **Pressure Point Attack** Hakusa targets a foe's pressure points to debilitate them. Hakusa makes a fist Strike. On a hit, the target takes damage as normal and must attempt a DC 35 Fortitude save. Regardless of the result of the save, the target is temporarily immune to Pressure Point Attack for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target's muscles become temporarily weaker. For 1 round, the target is [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and must succeed at a DC 5 flat check when attempting to use actions with the [manipulate](/Actions.aspx?ID=639) trait or the action is lost.
**Failure** As success, but the duration is 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the duration is 1 minute, the flat check is DC 11, and the target also takes –10-foot status penalty to their Speed for the duration. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** Hakusa's Strikes deal an additional 3d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
name: Shino Hakusa

He can still disrupt a triggering [manipulate](/Actions.aspx?ID=639) action if he critically succeeds his attempt.

**Damage** 3d10+10 bludgeoning

**Body Slam** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** Ran-to has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** Ran-to throws his foe against the ground. The grabbed creature takes 8d6 bludgeoning damage and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). The grapple ends.

**Leaping Catch** <actions string="Two Actions" />  Ran-to makes a mighty leap and attempts to Grab a foe while he soars. He makes a [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36), or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37). His maximum distance is 40 feet. He must succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to perform the leap as usual, but regardless of the type of leap, the DC is equal to the number of feet he moves in the air. Ran-to can attempt an Athletics check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) at any point during the jump. Immediately after the attempt, he (and the creature, if the Grapple was successful) falls to the ground. Neither creature takes damage from this fall.

**Titan Wrestler**   Ran-to can attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41), [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) creatures up to two sizes larger than him.

**Whirlwind Toss** <actions string="Three Actions" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** Ran-to has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** Ran-to spins the creature he's holding, smashing them against any creatures within his reach, and then throws the creature aside. Ran-to attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the grabbed creature's Fortitude DC. **Critical Success** Ran-to tosses his foe a great distance. The grapple ends. The grabbed creature is thrown into a space within 10 feet, takes 10d6 bludgeoning damage, and falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). All creatures adjacent to Ran-to take the same amount of bludgeoning damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). **Success** As critical success, except the grabbed creatures is thrown into a space within 5 feet, and creatures take 6d6 bludgeoning damage. **Failure** Ran-to tosses his foe aside. The grapple ends. The grabbed creature falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). **Critical Failure** Ran-to loses hold of his foe and the grapple ends.

</column>

markdown:
Gristleburst grew up among the Gullcracker goblins outside of Otari, but he was always a loner. His irrepressible curiosity and keen mind for chemistry earned him few friends among the goblins, even though his firebombs were always a source of good fun. When the Gullcrackers were killed and reanimated as [zombies](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=103), Gristleburst took on the lonely, sad task of putting down each one of his former kin. He now wanders the world as an alchemist-for-hire, just as eager to investigate unusual mysteries as he is to set things ablaze. Gristleburst is an unreliable ally. He's quick to make friends—particularly because he's otherwise all alone in the world—but he frequently pursues his own ideals without consulting his allies and hurls his bombs with reckless glee. Plot HooksGame Masters might consider the following plot hooks as a means of integrating Gristleburst into any Pathfinder Second Edition game.
  • When the heroes are following a treasure map through a dangerous wilderness, Gristleburst agrees to show them a safe path in exchange for a share of the loot.
  • The heroes are hunting an arsonist, and Gristleburst can help identify blast patterns and accelerants the arsonist used to set the blaze.
  • Gristleburst's reckless alchemy inadvertently sets a stand of trees or a building on fire, and he needs urgent help to put the fire out before it spreads.
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Gristleburst](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1477) **Source** [No-Prep Character: Gristleburst](/Sources.aspx?ID=91) **Perception** +7 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6) [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +7 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** +4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +0 **Infused Items** Gristleburst carries the following infused items, which last for 24 hours or until fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=75) without attempting a Crafting check. It remains potent only until the end of his next turn. When he throws this bomb, in addition to its normal effects, it creates a cloud of smoke in a 10-foot radius burst. Creatures in that area have the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition, and other creatures are concealed to them. This smoke lasts for 1 minute or until dissipated by a strong wind.

markdown:
Born in 7195 IC (4695 AR) to the noble Nai family of Goka, Yan Fei's comfortable childhood years were cut short when her parents fell to the blade of assassins during a diplomatic mission, leaving Yan Fei in the care of her grandfather, Nai Hu Buo, then the emperor of Goka. For the next 10 years, Hu Buo focused his time and efforts on Yan Fei's care. He trained her in the ins and outs of courtly life, shaping her into a suitable heir to the Nai legacy. Fearing future attempts on Yan Fei's life, Hu Buo also made sure Yan Fei could hold her own, and he tasked the [Butterfly Blades](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=91)— an elite group of warriors that served the Nai family as bodyguards—to train Yan Fei in martial arts and self-defense. The Butterfly Blades' storied history with the Nai family and Goka made them experts in espionage and intimidation, skills that Yan Fei learned and mastered alongside her grandfather's political expertise. By the age of 12, Yan Fei was fully trained in the arts of both combat and public affairs. Satisfied with his granddaughter's skill, Hu Buo returned to the world of Gokan politics, receiving an exuberant welcome from the people of Goka. Shortly thereafter, Hu Buo earned an unprecedented third term as emperor of Goka. Meanwhile, Yan Fei continued her training with the Butterfly Blades. Shortly after turning 14, Yan Fei vowed to find her family's killers. She spent several months in Goka proper, adopting multiple personas to dig up details from the city's unscrupulous information brokers. Her skills did ultimately lead her to her parents' assassins, whom she struck down with little effort, but she remained unsatisfied. Her continued efforts led her to the Yun-Ta clan, the Gokan noble family that contracted her parents' assassination. She needn't have bothered to seek out the Yun-Ta, for the family had made dozens of enemies since the Fei assassination and long ago sealed their own fate; by the time Yan Fei found the connection, the entire family had been dead for several years. Yan Fei's journey was over, and she had nothing to show for it. She returned home to continue her training as a noble, no longer enchanted by illusions of revenge. Two years into his third term, Hu Buo suddenly grew ill and died, leading Goka's parliament to fracture over disagreements of a suitable replacement. Yan Fei then stepped in, offering to complete her grandfather's term. The parliament attempted to block the suggestion, but Yan Fei called upon an obscure law she had studied as a child, which allowed for the inheritance of the position. Their hands tied, the parliament agreed and Yan Fei became acting empress in 7210 IC (4710 AR) at the age of 15. Yan Fei proved not only an effective leader, but a beloved figurehead who inspired Gokans both rich and poor, high-born and commoner alike. Yan Fei's aristocratic upbringing allowed her to slip into the politics with ease, and her experience hiding among the people during her time with the Butterfly Blades opened her eyes to the many plights of the everyday citizen. She used her position to push for change and progress for Goka, earning supporters from all corners. Although the next election for Goka's leader is still over a decade away, it seems the city's parliament and people are more than willing to accept Yan Fei's continued rule and reelect her as empress when the time comes. Campaign Role The player characters get their first chance to meet the empress and make a good impression on the powerful ruler in Chapter 3 of this adventure. Nai Yan Fei continues to serve as an important figure in the following adventures as a potential guide and information-provider, and the characters' interaction with her on Bonmu will determine what kind of relationship they have with the empress going forward. If the characters manage to win her over, they'll meet the kind aristocrat who's rightly earned the admiration of Goka's common folk. On the other hand, if their impression proves less than favorable, the characters must instead contend with a calculating politician who deftly navigates the minefields of diplomacy and etiquette—a ruthless empress who knows how to make the most of a group like the player characters before casting them aside when their worth has run its course. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 50 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 48 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 [Nai Yan Fei](/NPCs.aspx?ID=1478) **Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 91 **Perception** +36 **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10), [Tengu](/Languages.aspx?ID=47), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=43) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +37, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +36, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +36, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +38, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +36, [Goka Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +38, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +38, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +38, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +36, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +32, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +38, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +35, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +35, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +36 **Str** +1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +7 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [butterfly sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=162)_, _[greater hat of disguise](/Equipment.aspx?ID=442)_, _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [aristocrat's clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=15)_ --- **AC** 44 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +34 **Will** +33 **HP** 375 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _butterfly sword_ +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=190), [Twin](/Traits.aspx?ID=197)), **Damage** 4d4+13 slashing **Debilitating Strike** **Trigger** Nai Yan Fei hits a [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creature and deals damage; **Effect** Nai Yan Fei applies one of the following debilitations, which lasts until the end of her next turn.
  • **Debilitation** The target takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds.
  • **Debilitation** The target becomes [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13).
  • **Debilitation** The target can't flank other creatures, and it can't provide flanking for its allies.
  • **Debilitation** The target doesn't gain a circumstance bonus to AC from [Raising a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98), lesser cover, or standard cover; it gains only a +2 circumstance bonus to AC from greater cover or [Taking Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=90).
**Master Strike** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) **Trigger** Nai Yan Fei hits a [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creature and deals damage; **Effect** The target attempts a DC 39 Fortitude save. It then becomes immune to Nai Yan Fei's Master Strike for 1 day.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) until the end of Nai Yan Fei's next turn.
**Failure** The target is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) for 4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The target is paralyzed for 4 rounds, knocked [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) for 2 hours, or killed (Nai Yan Fei's choice). **Quick Change** Nai Yan Fei adopts a mundane disguise, appearing as a nondescript human with a neutral alignment and a mundane occupation such as common laborer, farmer, or peasant. She can return to her typical persona as empress at any time by using Quick Change again. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** Nai Yan Fei's Strikes deal an additional 4d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
name: Nai Yan Fei

Sepids are the brutal warlords of the divs, delighting in violence against mortals and their own particular flavor of vengeance. Inveterate liars, sepids can't help but spout the most outrageous fabrications, and their form and nature represent the power of falsehoods to snowball into violence that can cost countless lives. Among mortals, they rally troops to incite uprisings, rebellion, war, and other forms of carnage, savoring the bloodbaths when mere mortals slaughter each other. These giant beings usually stand around 13 feet tall; they wade through battlefields seeking out heroes and generals, rejoicing devilishly as they cut their foes down. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Sepid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4342) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 113 **Perception** +24; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +23, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +28, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +26, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +23 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +8 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [falchion](/Weapons.aspx?ID=373)_ --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +23 **Will** +20 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 350 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 10, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10 **Blatant Liar** While all divs delight in lying, sepids are compulsive and predictable liars who always do the opposite of what they claim they'll do. If a sepid is ever forced or compelled to tell the truth, they take 4d8 mental damage.
**Deflecting Lie** **Trigger** A creature hits the sepid with a ranged Strike or a ranged spell attack; **Effect** The sepid lies in an attempt to divert the attack. They roll a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check against the triggering creature's [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) DC. On a success, if the triggering attack roll was a success, it becomes a failure, and if the triggering attack roll was a critical hit, it becomes a normal success.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** A sepid gains an extra reaction each round that they can use only to make a Reactive Strike. --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** _falchion_ +30 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d10+16 slashing plus 1d6 mental **Melee** claw +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d6+16 slashing plus 1d6 mental **Divine Innate Spells** DC 34 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **3rd** [Translate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) (at will; self only) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) (at will), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **7th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), [Veil of Privacy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739) (at will; self only) **Rituals** DC 34 - **1st** [Div Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=46) - **2nd** [Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10) (no secondary caster required) **Rain of Debris** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The sepid calls forth a vicious, torrential hail of stone, wood, metal, and similar debris in a 40-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), dealing 10d6 bludgeoning damage and 5d6 spirit damage. Each creature in the area other than the sepid must attempt a DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex saving throw. The sepid can't use Rain of Debris again for 1d4 rounds.
Sepid Deceptions Given that sepids always do the opposite of what they say they will, it might seem difficult for these divs to deceive anyone, which is far from the truth. Often, sepids avoid making statements about their own intentions and instead give orders or speak in analogies, riddles, and anecdotes, allowing those they manipulate to parse out their rationales with clever forms of deception.
name: Sepid

markdown:
A close-knit settlement is rocked to its core when a beloved pillar of the community is accused of a heinous crime. The accused protest their innocence, but overwhelming evidence leaves no room for doubt. As justice is served, the community is irreversibly changed, its neighborly atmosphere giving way to lingering paranoia—the favored fare of the divoynik. Divoyniks are malevolent shapeshifters, capable not only of mimicking the physical appearance of other creatures but also of plucking thoughts and memories from their victims' minds. A divoynik retains their most recently assumed guise even after death. Spells like ring of truth help see through a divoynik, and truesight reveals its shapeshifted nature, making clear otherwise imperceptible markings similar to an animal's stripes on a divoynik's body. Beyond magic, people who encounter a divoynik can always rely on one other method of identification. Although identical to their mimicked form in all other respects, a divoynik's body contains neither blood nor a heart. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Divoynik](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4343) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 116 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155); two other languages; [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +9, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +9, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10 **Str** +3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +10 **Will** +11 **HP** 45 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) **Weaknesses** cracked mirror 3 **Cracked Mirror** A divoynik has weakness 3 to physical attacks made by a creature whose form they're mimicking, and the mimicked creature gains weakness 3 to physical attacks made by the divoynik.
**Sudden Betrayal** A divoynik can always use [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) when rolling initiative, as long as at least one enemy doesn't know their true nature. On the first round of combat, if the divoynik rolled Deception for initiative, creatures that haven't acted are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the divoynik.
**Savor Anguish** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet of the divoynik fails a saving throw against an emotion effect; **Effect** The divoynik feeds on their victim's mental distress, gaining 5 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) for up to 1 minute. They can feed on a given creature's emotions only once every 24 hours. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d10+5 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 20 - **3rd** [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (at will) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The divoynik can take on the specific appearance of any Small or Medium animal or humanoid they've seen. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes, but it might change the damage type their Strikes deal (such as to slashing with a claw). **Window to the Soul** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The divoynik meets the gaze of a creature within 60 feet whose form they've taken with their Change Shape ability. The target must attempt a DC 20 Will saving throw.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) for 1 minute. Hostile actions do not end this fascination, but if the divoynik Changes Shape, moves out of range, or is no longer visible to the target, the fascination immediately ends. While fascinated, the target takes a –1 circumstance penalty to Will saves against the divoynik's spells and abilities.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the divoynik can spend a free action to telepathically extract the answer to one question from the target. The target can attempt a DC 20 [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check to attempt to evade the query and give misleading information.
Transient Tormentors A divoynik delights in terrorizing their victims, typically taking calculated steps to reveal themself to their intended target long before the divoynik makes their move. Perhaps they allow the target a fleeting glimpse of their face in a crowd or a trace of movement from the corner of their eye, only to vanish before certainty can set in. In addition to providing the divoynik with opportunities to steal their victim's memories, this prolonged psychological assault often drives their victim into a state of extreme distrust and agitation, making the victim's apparent crimes all the more believable.
name: Divoynik

markdown:
The aberrations known as doprillus are hulks with banded muscles who wear ornate masks at all times, which fill the wearer with magical strength and fighting spirit. Doprillus love to battle, especially by grappling, and are eager to start brawls. On neutral ground, a doprillu offers to duel the strongestlooking opponent, but when a doprillu's home turf is invaded, no rules apply to the confrontation—the doprillu attacks with abandon any creature in their way. As befits the superheated blood that fuels them, doprillus make their homes in warm locations: hot jungles, sunny deserts, and underground caverns near sulfur vents. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Doprillu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4344) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 117 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +28 **Will** +24 +2 status to all saves vs. [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) **HP** 260 , regeneration [](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=72) 20 (deactivated by cold) **Immunities** [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (while wearing their mask), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (while wearing their mask) **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 15 **Mask of Power** A doprillu's unique, self-created wooden mask is the source of much of their power. A doprillu deprived of their mask loses their regeneration and immunity to [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92), and they immediately become enfeebled 1. The enfeebled value increases by 1 at the start of each of the doprillu's turns, to a maximum of enfeebled 4. If the mask is put back on, the doprillu immediately regains their abilities and loses the enfeebled condition. A creature can pull off a doprillu's mask with a successful DC 34 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check.
**Volcanic Veins** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) Fiery magma runs through the doprillu's veins burning those in their clutches. A creature that starts its turn grabbed by the doprillu takes 7d6 fire damage.
**Deflect Arrow** **Trigger** The doprillu is the target of a physical ranged attack; **Requirements** The doprillu is aware of the attack, isn't [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against it, and has a hand free; **Effect** The doprillu gains a +4 circumstance bonus to its AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 40 feet **Melee** fist +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d8+16 bludgeoning plus 2d6 fire and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 28 - **Constant (6th)** [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) **Body Strike** **Requirements** The doprillu has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77); **Effect** The doprillu swings the grabbed creature as a weapon. This [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) has a +30 attack modifier and deals 3d8+16 bludgeoning damage. The Strike is magical and has a reach of 10 feet. On a hit, the grabbed creature takes half the damage dealt to the target. **Whirlwind Throw** **Requirements** The doprillu has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77); **Effect** The doprillu whirls the grabbed creature about, making a separate Body Strike against each creature in reach. After making a Strike against all creatures in reach, the doprillu can hurl the grabbed creature up to 50 feet as a ranged Strike. This Strike has the same attack modifier and damage as Body Strike, but it has the [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711) weapon trait. Apply the doprillu's multiple attack penalty to the Strikes normally.
Doprillu Masks A doprillu’s stylized mask isn’t a part of the creature themself, though doprillus inherently understand the mask’s purpose and power. When born, doprillus start out weak and small. Once one matures and gains sufficient intelligence and manual dexterity, they carve a personal mask. The first time they don the mask, a doprillu rushes out to seek a fight. This mask is never replaced, and it might be marred from hundreds of battles. Beneath the mask is a hauntingly blank face with tiny eyes and a pitch-black mouth, lacking any other features.
name: Doprillu

Among the largest and fiercest dragons, cinder dragons are typically volatile, demanding respect—even deference—from lesser creatures. Cinder dragons' appearance evokes their flame, often in scales with mixed patterns of red, orange, and yellow. Many cinder dragons dwell in active volcanoes and similarly fiery locales. Cinder dragons prefer treasures that can withstand the heat of their bodies and lairs, with gemstones, gold, and silver common among their hoards. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Young Cinder Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4345) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 118 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet, smoke vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +21, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair a cinder dragon's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +17 **Will** +19 +2 status to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 210 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 27
**Boiling Blood** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a melee attack; **Effect** The dragon's superheated blood spills onto the attacker. The target takes 6d6 fire damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

Creatures that critically fail their save catch fire, taking 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The area then fills with black smoke for 1 minute. This has the effects of mist, except it fills the cone's area. The dragon can't use Pyre Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Stoke the Flames** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon intensifies nearby fires. Every foe within 60 feet that is taking [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) takes 3d6 fire damage.

markdown:
Coral dragons are the primal guardians of the vast living coral reefs found in oceans across Golarion. Even by draconic standards, coral dragons stand out for their arrogance and vanity, boasting inordinate pride in the vibrant hues of their own coral-encrusted scales and the dazzling splendor of the reefs they protect. Coral dragons like to decorate their reefs, which they view as lairs, with objects of beauty, dotted with the occasional bit of valuables like coins. Those who seek the favor of a coral dragon often find the creature easily swayed by flattery, particularly when paired with shiny or brightly colored gifts that complement the scintillating beauty of the dragon and their underwater domain. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Young Coral Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4348) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 120 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=84) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +18, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +16, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +16 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +14 **Will** +15 +2 status to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 135 **Immunities** [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Reef Bond** Every coral dragon is mystically bound to a single living coral reef. If the dragon moves more than 3 miles from their reef, they become sickened 1 and unable to recover, with the sickened value increasing by 1 every 6 hours unless they succeed at a DC 28 Fortitude save. After 24 hours, the dragon becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1; its drained value increases by 1 every 24 hours.
If the dragon's reef suffers significant damage, they immediately become aware of the location where the reef was harmed but not the source or nature of the damage. Should the reef ever be completely destroyed, the dragon is immediately slain.
**Biomineralize** **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit by a melee weapon without reach or an unarmed attack that deals slashing or piercing damage; **Effect** A gout of blood spurts from the dragon's wound and instantaneously calcifies into a jagged branch of sharpened coral. The coral branch impales the triggering creature, dealing 5d6 piercing damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The triggering creature also takes 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) on a critical failure. Regardless of the outcome, the coral then crumbles to dust. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 40 feet, swim 80 feet **Melee** jaws +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+10 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+10 slashing **Melee** tail +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+10 bludgeoning plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Hydraulic Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Hydraulic Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The dragon exhales a pressurized jet of water that deals 9d6 bludgeoning damage in an 80-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Creatures that critically fail their Reflex save against the Hydraulic Breath are pushed back 10 feet and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). The dragon can't use Hydraulic Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Reef Meld** ([Extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=596), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The coral dragon is in physical contact with their bound reef; **Effect** The dragon physically merges with the reef and vanishes, along with up to two willing creatures, into an extradimensional space where it can neither affect nor be affected by the outside world. The effect lasts indefinitely or until the dragon Dismisses it. Once merged, the dragon can spend 1 minute traveling to and emerging from any point on its reef up to 1 mile away.
name: Young Coral Dragon

The coral formations covering the dragon's body glow and shimmer with vivid colors, overwhelming the senses and forcing any creature entering or beginning their turn in the aura to attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save. Regardless of the outcome, the creature is temporarily immune to the dragon's kaleidoscopic display for 1 minute.

**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 round and dazzled for 1 minute.
**Reef Bond** Every coral dragon is mystically bound to a single living coral reef. If the dragon moves more than 3 miles from their reef, they become sickened 1 and unable to recover, with the sickened value increasing by 1 every 6 hours unless they succeed at a DC 34 Fortitude save. After 24 hours, the dragon becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1; its drained value increases by 1 every 24 hours.
If the dragon's reef suffers significant damage, they immediately become aware of the location where the reef was harmed but not the source or nature of the damage. Should the reef ever be completely destroyed, the dragon is immediately slain.
**Biomineralize** **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit by a melee weapon without reach or an unarmed attack that deals slashing or piercing damage; **Effect** A gout of blood spurts from the dragon's wound and instantaneously calcifies into a jagged branch of sharpened coral. The triggering creature also takes 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) on a critical failure. Creatures that critically fail their Reflex save against the Hydraulic Breath are pushed back 10 feet and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). The dragon can't use Hydraulic Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Reef Meld** ([Extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=596), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The coral dragon is in physical contact with their bound reef; **Effect** The dragon physically merges with the reef and vanishes, along with up to four willing creatures, into an extradimensional space where it can neither affect nor be affected by the outside world. markdown:
Fear is one of the most powerful emotions, and despair dragons are masters of channeling those feelings of terror and hopelessness for their own benefit. As with other occult dragons, despair dragons are driven by an innate compulsion, in their case, the desire to strike terror in others. Despair dragons tend to settle near settlements, so they have a source of fear to draw from. The most common items in despair dragon hoards are scrolls, tomes, and relics that serve as research material. Through these, despair dragons learn of local folklore, urban legends, and more to better haunt their targets. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Young Despair Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4351) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 122 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), fearsense (imprecise) 60 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 60 feet[_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +16, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Fearsense** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon senses all creatures with the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition at the listed range. --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +18 **Will** +20 +2 status to all saves vs. [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) **HP** 140 **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet, DC 28
**Consume Fear** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet loses the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition; **Effect** The dragon feasts upon the fear that leaves the triggering creature's body, gaining 4d8 temp[orary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 minute.
**Unbidden Thoughts** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a weapon or unarmed attack; **Effect** The attacker's mind fills with visions of their worst fears that overwhelm their senses, and they must choose one of the following results: either the triggering attack becomes a normal success, or the critical hit is unaffected but the triggering creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** jaws +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+12 piercing **Melee** claws +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 5 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+12 slashing **Melee** tail +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+10 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 28 - **4th** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (at will) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Shrieking Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Shrieking Breath** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765)) The dragon lets out a cacophonous sound made of every scream the dragon has drawn from a terrified enemy, dealing 8d6 sonic damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). Creatures who fail their Will save must spend the first action of their next turn doing nothing but screaming. The dragon can't use Shrieking Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Young Despair Dragon

**Consume Fear** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet loses the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition; **Effect** The dragon feasts upon the fear that leaves the triggering creature's body, gaining 5d8 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 minute.
**Unbidden Thoughts** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a weapon or unarmed attack; **Effect** The attacker's mind fills with visions of their worst fears that overwhelm their senses, and they must choose one of the following results: either the triggering attack becomes a normal success, or the critical hit is unaffected but the triggering creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 150 feet **Melee** jaws +25 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+16 piercing **Melee** claws +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+16 slashing **Melee** tail +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d6+14 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 33 - **6th** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (at will) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Shrieking Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Shrieking Breath** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765)) The dragon lets out a cacophonous sound made of every scream the dragon has drawn from a terrified enemy, dealing 12d6 sonic damage in a 40-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). Creatures who fail their Will save must spend the first action of their next turn doing nothing but screaming.
