This process takes 1 minute to complete, during which the dragon is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). If the dragon stops or is interrupted in this process, the bladders deflate, and the dragon must start over. Once the process is complete, the dragon can maintain the inflated bladders indefinitely, and can Dismiss to deflate the bladders and retract its tongue instantly.
The inflated tongue takes the basic form of an animal or humanoid and can be inflated to be either Small or Medium. The form resembles the general silhouette of a creature, though closer inspection and success at a DC 30 [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) check can determine the true nature of the tongue. While inflated, the dragon can send its voice through the decoy, though keeping its tongue inflated makes it difficult to speak, causing the dragon to take a –4 circumstance penalty to any checks related to speaking, such as [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389).
The dragon's tongue can extend up to 90 feet from the dragon's body and it can fully extend its tongue as part of the process to inflate the bladders. The dragon can move the inflated part of its tongue up to 15 feet at a time with an action, which has the concentrate, manipulate, and move traits. While extended, the inflated end of the tongue occupies space as a creature of the appropriate size, but the rest of the tongue doesn't impede or block movement in any way. The dragon's scent functions through cilia at the end of the tongue, but otherwise the dragon has no means of knowing what's near its tongue.
Attacking the tongue is the same as attacking the dragon, except that the tongue is always off-guard. If the tongue takes any damage, it immediately deflates and remains out. The dragon remains off-guard as long as its tongue is out, but the dragon can retract its tongue with two consecutive [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions. If the tongue takes damage, the dragon can't use its tongue decoy again for 1 day.
name: Adult Despair Dragon

Despair dragons tend to settle near settlements, so they have a source of fear to draw from. The most common items in despair dragon hoards are scrolls, tomes, and relics that serve as research material. Through these, despair dragons learn of local folklore, urban legends, and more to better haunt their targets. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Ancient Despair Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4353) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 124 **Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), fearsense (precise) 60 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 90 feet, [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +31, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +33, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +32, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +35, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +29, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +29, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +33 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +5 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +8 **Fearsense** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon senses all creatures with the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition at the listed range. --- **AC** 41 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +30 **Will** +32 +2 status to all saves vs. [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) **HP** 325 **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet, DC 40
**Consume Fear** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet loses the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition; **Effect** The dragon feasts upon the fear that leaves the triggering creature's body, gaining 7d8 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 minute.
**Unbidden Thoughts** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a weapon or unarmed attack; **Effect** The attacker's mind fills with visions of their worst fears that overwhelm their senses, and they must choose one of the following results: either the triggering attack becomes a normal success, or the critical hit is unaffected but the triggering creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 180 feet **Melee** jaws +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+20 piercing **Melee** claws +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+20 slashing **Melee** tail +31 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+18 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 40 - **7th** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (at will), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Shrieking Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Look Behind You** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Trigger** The dragon successfully [Hides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) from a creature within 90 feet; **Effect** The dragon teleports to a space directly behind the target creature. The dragon immediately becomes detected unless their chosen position has cover or another means to remain [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79). **Shrieking Breath** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765)) The dragon lets out a cacophonous sound made of every scream the dragon has drawn from a terrified enemy, dealing 17d6 sonic damage in a 50-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). Creatures who fail their Will save must spend the first action of their next turn doing nothing but screaming. The dragon can't use Shrieking Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Tongue Decoy** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The despair dragon inflates several bladders at the end of its tongue to create the basic form of a creature. This process takes 1 minute to complete, during which the dragon is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). If the dragon stops or is interrupted in this process, the bladders deflate, and the dragon must start over. Once the process is complete, the dragon can maintain the inflated bladders indefinitely, and can Dismiss to deflate the bladders and retract its tongue instantly.
The inflated tongue takes the basic form of an animal or humanoid and can be inflated to be either Small or Medium. The form resembles the general silhouette of a creature, though closer inspection and success at a DC 35 [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) check can determine the true nature of the tongue. While inflated, the dragon can send its voice through the decoy, though keeping its tongue inflated makes it difficult to speak, causing the dragon to take a –4 circumstance penalty to any checks related to speaking, such as [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389).
The dragon's tongue can extend up to 90 feet from the dragon's body and it can fully extend its tongue as part of the process to inflate the bladders. The dragon can move the inflated part of its tongue up to 15 feet at a time with an action, which has the concentrate, manipulate, and move traits. While extended, the inflated end of the tongue occupies space as a creature of the appropriate size, but the rest of the tongue doesn't impede or block movement in any way. The dragon's scent functions through cilia at the end of the tongue, but otherwise the dragon has no means of knowing what's near its tongue.
Attacking the tongue is the same as attacking the dragon, except that the tongue is always off-guard. If the tongue takes any damage, it immediately deflates and remains out. The dragon remains off-guard as long as its tongue is out, but the dragon can retract its tongue with two consecutive [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions. If the tongue takes damage, the dragon can't use its tongue decoy again for 1 day.
name: Ancient Despair Dragon

markdown:
No place can contain a phase dragon or even hold their interest for long; their innate arcane connection ties them to teleportation and repositioning magic. Explorers and scholars, phase dragons move about at will, discovering new locales and the arcane secrets of teleportation. They frequently establish multiple lairs in far-flung places they repeatedly visit. Beyond the typical wealth found in lairs, phase dragons tend to keep items of sentimental value from their travels, such as a particularly rare flower from the region or a piece by a local artist. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Young Phase Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4354) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 124 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +19, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +18, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +22, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +18, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +17 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +6 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +20 **Will** +19 +2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) **HP** 120 **Immunities** [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Unerring Location** The dragon automatically attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) effect that targets them (counteract rank 5th, counteract modifier +20). The dragon can choose to be affected normally instead. Other creatures targeted by the same effect remain affected normally.
**Shoo!** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Trigger** An enemy within 15 feet damages the dragon; **Effect** The dragon teleports the creature up to 15 feet away. The destination must be on the ground and in a space with no hazards. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** jaws +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+8 piercing **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+8 slashing **Melee** tail +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+8 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 28 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **4th** [Flicker](/Spells.aspx?ID=1532), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **5th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) **Dislocating Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The dragon exhales a swirl of energy that pulls creatures apart, dealing 8d6 force damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The dragon can teleport any creature that fails its save, teleporting that creature up to 30 feet (or twice as far on a critical failure) in any direction. The destination must be on the ground and in a space with no hazards. The dragon can't use Dislocating Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** Whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike, the dragon chooses to either recharge Dislocating Breath or regain an expended teleportation spell. **Phase Jump** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The dragon teleports up to 60 feet. If they are airborne, they maintain their momentum, and do not fall at the end of their turn, even if they didn't use an action to [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312).
name: Young Phase Dragon

markdown:
Requiem dragons are stewards of the River of Souls and the process through which souls reach their final destination in the afterlife. The safe journey of a soul is of utmost importance to a requiem dragon, and some will follow individual souls from their first entry into the river through to their judgment in the Boneyard and eventually to their ultimate resting place. Most requiem dragons tie themselves to specific planes and shepherd any souls bound to that plane, leading to dragons linked to places like [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41) or [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), though these dragons never swear true allegiance to these planes. markdown:
Resurrection dragons teeter between life and death. They have a mastery of vital energies, allowing them to restore life to the dead, and a mastery of void energies, to bestow death on others. They make use of their abilities to play with the lives of mortals, calling on spirits to aid them or reviving creatures they find important or interesting. The lair of a resurrection dragon is generally a barren place. While they still hoard wealth like other dragons, they do little to decorate their lairs and treasures are generally kept in dark niches, as if the dragon has little care for their possessions. Resurrection dragons tend to take tokens from those they resurrect or plan to resurrect, however, and these are kept particularly safe. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Young Resurrection Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4360) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 129 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +18, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +18, [Necromancy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +14 **Will** +19 **HP** 140 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), sleep **Resistances** [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) 10 **Risen Commander** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A resurrection dragon has a strong connection with its minions and can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) [_summon undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706) or [_invoke spirits_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578) as a free action once per turn.
**Reawaken!** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Trigger** A living creature the resurrection dragon can see dies; **Effect** The resurrection dragon uses divine and vital energy to retether the soul to its dead body. The willing creature is returned to life with the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) 1 condition at the start of its next turn. A creature can be resurrected by this ability only once.
**Siphon Life** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet uses a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) effect that restores Hit Points; **Effect** The resurrection dragon redirects vital energies away from the effect, minimizing its impact. The triggering effect results in the minimum amount on any dice rolls to restore Hit Points, and any flat values for restoring Hit Points (such as the additional Hit Points for a two-action [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) spell) are cut in half. The dragon then gains 1d8 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 round. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** jaws +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+9 piercing plus 1d6 void **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+9 slashing **Melee** tail +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+9 bludgeoning **Divine Innate Spells** DC 26 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **4th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×2), [Summon Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706) (at will), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw strikes and one tail strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Soul Siphoning Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Soul Siphoning Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The dragon unleashes a torrent of divine energy, dealing 7d6 void damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save) that draws the life force from creatures within. The dragon gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 5 until their Soul Siphoning Breath recharges. The resurrection dragon can't use Soul Siphoning Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Young Resurrection Dragon

Resurrection dragons tend to take tokens from those they resurrect or plan to resurrect, however, and these are kept particularly safe. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Adult Resurrection Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4361) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 129 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +20, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +23, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +25, [Necromancy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +26, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +22 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +20 **Will** +25 **HP** 230 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) 15 **Risen Commander** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A resurrection dragon has a strong connection with its minions and can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) [_summon undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706) or [_invoke spirits_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578) as a free action once per turn.
**Reawaken!** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Trigger** A living creature the resurrection dragon can see dies; **Effect** The resurrection dragon uses divine and vital energy to retether the soul to its dead body. The willing creature is returned to life stabilized at 0 HP. A creature can be resurrected by this ability only once.
**Siphon Life** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet uses a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) effect that restores Hit Points; **Effect** The resurrection dragon redirects vital energies away from the effect, minimizing its impact. The triggering effect results in the minimum amount on any dice rolls to restore Hit Points, and any flat values for restoring Hit Points (such as the additional Hit Points for a two-action [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) spell) are cut in half. The dragon then gains 2d8 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 round. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 160 feet **Melee** jaws +26 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+11 piercing plus 1d6 void **Melee** claw +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d8+11 slashing **Melee** tail +24 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+11 bludgeoning **Divine Innate Spells** DC 32 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **4th** [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712) (at will) - **5th** [Invoke Spirits](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578) (×2) - **6th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×2), [Raise Dead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645), [Summon Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706) (at will) **Arise!** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623)) The resurrection dragon uses their mastery over life energy to cast their own soul into the Boneyard and pull a willing creature's soul back to its body in a process that takes 1 hour. This has the effects of [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645), except the maximum level of the target is 8th and the soul is tethered to the dragon's. Only one creature can be tethered to the dragon's soul at a time. If the creature and the dragon are no longer on the same plane or the dragon dies, the raised creature dies and can't be raised with Arise! again. The dragon can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the connection at any time. Doing so doesn't prevent the dragon from raising the creature with Arise! again.
While raised in this way, the creature is still a valid target for _raise dead_, [_resurrection_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=123), and similar effects. Returning the creature to life in this way fully restores the creature, severing the connection to the dragon and allowing the dragon to establish a connection with a different creature. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw strikes and one tail strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Soul Siphoning Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Soul Siphoning Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The dragon unleashes a torrent of divine energy, dealing 11d6 void damage in a 40-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save) that draws the life force from creatures within. The dragon gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 10 until their Soul Siphoning Breath recharges. The resurrection dragon can't use Soul Siphoning Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

markdown:
Resurrection dragons teeter between life and death. They have a mastery of vital energies, allowing them to restore life to the dead, and a mastery of void energies, to bestow death on others. They make use of their abilities to play with the lives of mortals, calling on spirits to aid them or reviving creatures they find important or interesting. The lair of a resurrection dragon is generally a barren place. While they still hoard wealth like other dragons, they do little to decorate their lairs and treasures are generally kept in dark niches, as if the dragon has little care for their possessions. Resurrection dragons tend to take tokens from those they resurrect or plan to resurrect, however, and these are kept particularly safe. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Ancient Resurrection Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4362) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 130 **Perception** +32; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +28, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +28, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +33, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +31, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +33, [Necromancy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +36, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +33, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +30 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +9 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +27 **Will** +32 **HP** 320 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) 20 **Risen Commander** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A resurrection dragon has a strong connection with its minions and can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) [_summon undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706) or [_invoke spirits_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578) as a free action once per turn.
**Reawaken!** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Trigger** A living creature the resurrection dragon can see dies; **Effect** The resurrection dragon uses divine and vital energy to retether the soul to its dead body. The willing creature is returned to life with half of their total Hit Points. A creature can be resurrected by this ability only once.
**Siphon Life** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet uses a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) effect that restores Hit Points; **Effect** The resurrection dragon redirects vital energies away from the effect, minimizing its impact. The triggering effect results in the minimum amount on any dice rolls to restore Hit Points, and any flat values for restoring Hit Points (such as the additional Hit Points for a two-action [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) spell) are cut in half. The dragon then gains 3d8 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 round. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 200 feet **Melee** jaws +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+15 piercing plus 1d6 void **Melee** claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+15 slashing **Melee** tail +31 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+15 bludgeoning **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **4th** [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712) (at will) - **9th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×2), [Invoke Spirits](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578) (×2), [Raise Dead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645), [Summon Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706) (at will) **Arise!** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623)) The resurrection dragon uses their mastery over life energy to cast their own soul into the Boneyard and pull a willing creature's soul back to its body in a process that takes 1 hour. This has the effects of [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645), except the maximum level of the target is 13th and the soul is tethered to the dragon's. Only one creature can be tethered to the dragon's soul at a time. If the creature and the dragon are no longer on the same plane or the dragon dies, the raised creature dies and can't be raised with Arise! again. The dragon can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the connection at any time. Doing so doesn't prevent the dragon from raising the creature with Arise! again.
While raised in this way, the creature is still a valid target for _raise dead_, [_resurrection_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=123), and similar effects. Returning the creature to life in this way fully restores the creature, severing the connection to the dragon and allowing the dragon to establish a connection with a different creature. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw strikes and one tail strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Soul Siphoning Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Necro Puppeteer** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The dragon siphons energy into an undead creature, a [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) creature, or a corpse they can see within 60 feet. The dragon moves the target creature 30 feet and causes it to unleash a wave of void energy in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), dealing 6d8 void damage (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). **Soul Siphoning Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The dragon unleashes a torrent of divine energy, dealing 16d6 void damage in a 50-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save) that draws the life force from creatures within. The dragon gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 15 until their Soul Siphoning Breath recharges. The resurrection dragon can't use Soul Siphoning Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

markdown:
Rune dragons have an innate insight on the power of runes. Most rune dragons keep a variety of runes etched on their scales, which their bodies can magically empower. They typically don't cast spells themselves, instead relying on their ability to create runes on the fly to produce desired effects. A rune dragon often refers to their hoard as their library, as it's full of important historical texts, plays, speeches, textbooks, and spellbooks written by people of different cultures in a variety of languages. While they can often speak a language with the aid of magic, they much prefer to learn languages in the context of their speakers so they can grasp the nuances hidden within the syntax and the magic within their symbols. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Young Rune Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4363) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 131 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), magic sense (imprecise) 60 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156); five additional common languages **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +19, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +21, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +18, [Linguistics Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +21, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +20, [Rune Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +23, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +19 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 **Magic Sense** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The rune dragon is aware of any active magical abilities and effects within the listed range. When the dragon [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301), it gains the benefits of a 4th-rank [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) spell within the listed range (in addition to the normal benefits of Seeking).
**Runic Scales** The rune dragon's scales function as [_runestones_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3003). The rune dragon can't use the effects or abilities of the runes etched on its scales, but they can transfer these runes to appropriate objects. Transferring a rune to or from an item in this way requires 1 minute, during which the dragon is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). The process is automatic and doesn't require a check, but if the dragon stops or is interrupted in this process, the rune is destroyed. A rune dragon can have any number of runes etched on its scales, though they typically have only a handful of runes etched on their scales at a time. **Items** [3 common runes of 5th level or lower etched upon their scales](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1770) --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +17 **Will** +21 +2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) **HP** 175 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), Shifting Runes, [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Retributive Rune** **Trigger** A creature within 15 feet damages the rune dragon; **Effect** With a burst of runic magic, the rune dragon uses their detonating rune ability on the triggering creature and immediately causes the rune to detonate if it didn't automatically do so. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 100 feet **Melee** jaws +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+10 piercing plus detonating rune **Melee** claw +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+10 slashing plus detonating rune **Melee** tail +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+10 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) 10 feet **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Runic Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Detonating Rune** The rune dragon's Strikes and abilities can leave a detonating rune on their targets. If a creature would receive a detonating rune while they already have one, instead of adding another rune, their current detonating rune activates, dealing 4d6 damage to the target and expending the rune. The detonating rune's damage type matches the dragon's current Shifting Rune. A creature can use an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to remove the rune. Detonating runes fade after 1 minute if not detonated. **Runic Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The dragon launches hundreds of exploding runes that detonate upon impact, dealing 9d6 damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage type of this ability is determined by Shifting Rune. Creatures who fail the save are also affected by detonating rune. The dragon can't use Runic Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Shifting Runes** The rune dragon chooses between [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage. T he runes etched upon the dragon shift, forming runes of that energy on its scales. The dragon gains immunity to that damage type, and their detonating runes and Runic Breath deal that damage type. Anyone trained in Arcana can immediately recognize the energy type of the etched rune without a check.
name: Young Rune Dragon

The rune can trap only a single creature at a time. The rune vanishes either when a creature succeeds against the rune, when a creature successfully Escapes the rune, or after 1 minute. A creature adjacent to the rune can use an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to remove the rune. **Runic Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The dragon launches hundreds of exploding runes that detonate upon impact, dealing 12d6 damage in a 40-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage type of this ability is determined by Shifting Rune. Creatures who fail the save are also affected by detonating rune. The dragon can't use Runic Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Shifting Runes** The rune dragon chooses between [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage. T he runes etched upon the dragon shift, forming runes of that energy on its scales. The dragon gains immunity to that damage type, and their detonating runes and Runic Breath deal that damage type. Anyone trained in Arcana can immediately recognize the energy type of the etched rune without a check.

summary_markdown: Rune dragons have an innate insight on the power of runes. Most rune dragons keep a variety of runes etched on their scales, which their bodies can …

markdown:
Rune dragons have an innate insight on the power of runes. Most rune dragons keep a variety of runes etched on their scales, which their bodies can magically empower. They typically don't cast spells themselves, instead relying on their ability to create runes on the fly to produce desired effects. A rune dragon often refers to their hoard as their library, as it's full of important historical texts, plays, speeches, textbooks, and spellbooks written by people of different cultures in a variety of languages. While they can often speak a language with the aid of magic, they much prefer to learn languages in the context of their speakers so they can grasp the nuances hidden within the syntax and the magic within their symbols. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Ancient Rune Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4365) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 133 **Perception** +34; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), magic sense (imprecise) 60 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156); all common languages, 5 additional languages **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +34, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +38, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +36, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +36, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +34, [Linguistics Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +36, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +36, [Rune Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +38, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +34, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +34 **Str** +8 **Dex** +8 **Con** +9 **Int** +10 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +5 **Magic Sense** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The rune dragon is aware of any active magical abilities and effects within the listed range. When the dragon [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301), it gains the benefits of a 4th-rank [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) spell within the listed range (in addition to the normal benefits of Seeking).
**Runic Scales** The rune dragon's scales function as [_runestones_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3003). The rune dragon can't use the effects or abilities of the runes etched on its scales, but they can transfer these runes to appropriate objects. Transferring a rune to or from an item in this way requires 1 minute, during which the dragon is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). The process is automatic and doesn't require a check, but if the dragon stops or is interrupted in this process, the rune is destroyed. A rune dragon can have any number of runes etched on its scales, though they typically have only a handful of runes etched on their scales at a time. **Items** [3 common runes of 9th level or lower etched upon their scales](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1770) --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +30 **Will** +34 +2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) **HP** 355 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), Shifting Runes, [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Canceling Rune** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Trigger** The dragon is the target of a spell that requires a saving throw; **Effect** The dragon attempts to unmake the spell's foundational runes. They attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the spell (counteract rank 10th, counteract modifier +33). If successful, the dragon is unaffected by the spell; other subjects are affected by the spell normally. The dragon can't use Canceling Rune again for 1d4 rounds.
**Retributive Rune** **Trigger** A creature within 15 feet damages the rune dragon; **Effect** With a burst of runic magic, the rune dragon uses their detonating rune ability on the triggering creature and immediately causes the rune to detonate if it didn't automatically do so. --- **Speed** 80 feet, fly 180 feet **Melee** jaws +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+16 piercing plus detonating rune **Melee** claw +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d6+16 slashing plus detonating rune **Melee** tail +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+16 bludgeoning plus [Improved Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=29) 15 feet **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Runic Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Detonating Rune** The rune dragon's Strikes and abilities can leave a detonating rune on their targets. If a creature would receive a detonating rune while they already have one, instead of adding another rune, their current detonating rune activates, dealing 6d6 damage to the target and expending the rune. The detonating rune's damage type matches the dragon's current Shifting Rune. A creature can use an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to remove the rune. Detonating runes fade after 1 minute if not detonated. **Entangling Rune** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The rune dragon creates a large trapping rune in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 60 feet. A creature other than the dragon that enters a trapped area or ends their turn in the trapped area activates the rune, causing it to entangle them. That creature must succeed at a DC 41 Reflex save or become [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for 1 minute or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296). The rune can trap only a single creature at a time. The rune vanishes either when a creature succeeds against the rune, when a creature successfully Escapes the rune, or after 1 minute. A creature adjacent to the rune can use an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to remove the rune. **Runic Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The dragon launches hundreds of exploding runes that detonate upon impact, dealing 17d6 damage in a 50-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage type of this ability is determined by Shifting Rune. Creatures who fail the save are also affected by detonating rune. The dragon can't use Runic Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Shifting Runes** The rune dragon chooses between [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage. T he runes etched upon the dragon shift, forming runes of that energy on its scales. The dragon gains immunity to that damage type, and their detonating runes and Runic Breath deal that damage type. Anyone trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) can immediately recognize the energy type of the etched rune without a check. **Trail of Runes** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The rune dragon Strides, leaving behind a collection of runes wherever it steps. The dragon chooses to leave behind either detonating runes or entangling runes. Every space that the dragon moves through, including spaces occupied by creatures, gains a rune. These runes automatically activate when a creature other than the rune dragon enters the space or ends its turn in the space. The runes can otherwise be removed or vanish like normal.
name: Ancient Rune Dragon

Whisper dragons are keen collectors of rumors and secrets who spend centuries honing their information networks and relationships. They generally do so not out of an intent to hold the information over others or to use for their own machinations, but simply because the process of learning and gathering information is fulfilling. Their hoards are sparse compared to those of other dragons, as they hold their true treasures—secrets—in their minds. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Young Whisper Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4366) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 133 **Perception** +15; **Distracting Whisper** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** The dragon is targeted with an attack; **Effect** A mysterious voice whispers something disconcerting in the triggering creature's ear, inflicting a –2 circumstance penalty to the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 200 feet **Melee** jaws +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+15 piercing **Melee** claw +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+15 slashing **Melee** tail +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+15 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 37 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) - **2nd** [Embed Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1511) - **3rd** [Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465) (at will), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (at will), [Ring of Truth](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656) - **4th** [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466) (at will) - **5th** [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601) - **6th** [Retrocognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652) - **8th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) - **Constant (8th)** [Hidden Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556) **Cogitation Breath** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon unleashes a befuddling miasma, dealing 15d6 mental damage in a 50-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). A creature that fails its save is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (stupefied 2 on a critical failure) for 1 minute. The dragon can't use Cogitation Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Cogitation Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Steal Knowledge** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon plucks a fragment of knowledge from the mind of a creature within 60 feet, choosing a skill to affect. The creature must attempt a DC 35 Will save. **Success** The creature is unaffected. **Failure** For the next minute, the creature takes a –1 status penalty to checks using that skill, and the dragon gets a +1 status bonus to using that skill. **Critical Failure** As failure, but the penalty is –2 and the bonus is +2. **Thought Whispers** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** The dragon sends their mind out to seek others' thoughts, affecting all creatures within 60 feet with [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (Will DC 37). **Unveil Secret** ([Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon delves into the mind of a creature within 60 feet to scour for secrets, learning something the creature would find embarrassing or shameful unless they succeed a DC 37 Will save. The target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 and can't reduce their frightened condition for 1 minute or until the dragon reveals the secret. As a reaction when the affected creature attempts a check, the dragon can reveal their secret to discomfit them, requiring them to roll twice and take the lower result; this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect.

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Dragonbloods Of Xa Hoi</title>

Long ago, the warring factions of what would become the Tian Xia nation of Xa Hoi pledged their fealty to the area’s [sovereign dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=230) in return for their protection . Since then, Xa Hoi has been ruled by those dragons’ descendants. The citizens are used to the sovereign dragon’s prevalence throughout the nation and consider physical evidence of the dragons’ influence in their offspring to be a desired blessing. Sorcerers with the [draconic](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=23) bloodline and [dragonblood](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=86) individuals are often held in high esteem and are expected to do great things.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[House Drake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4370)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Beautifully colored in purples and electric blues, the little dragonets called house drakes are genuinely brilliant in more ways than one. They're playful and kind, and while they have a long tradition of assisting spellcasters, they prefer to be treated as equals and partners rather than pets. They're quite intelligent and don't appreciate condescension from those who treat them as simple animals. 

These tiny dragons have appeared in traditional Varisian tales for centuries, but only relatively recently have they become known by the name “house drake.” This new name arose in the city of Korvosa, where house drakes have adapted particularly well to urban life. When students of the magic school called the Acadamae failed in their studies and allowed their imp familiars to run wild, these tiny dragons found that they were particularly well suited to combating them. The two species are well matched in cunning. Though their clashes typically begin with trying to outwit one another, they often end in messy brawls across rooftops and into alleys. The residents of Korvosa appreciated both the protection and the dragons' charming appearance and demeanor. As such, their population has flourished, and house drakes are more common in Korvosa than anywhere else in the world.

House drakes have peculiar grooming habits, often sharpening their teeth with silver coins or small jewelry. Because of this, their bites and exhalations are tinged with particles of silver. Otherwise, they're carnivorous, eating vermin and small birds. Though house drakes are capable of hunting for food, many of them find it a bit dull and will happily accept donations of food, preferring to spend their time at other pursuits. To truly get on a house drake's good side, though, one should offer a gift of silver. Donations to these dragonets have become regular practice in Korvosa, to the point where “paid any drakes lately?” has become a common phrase to suggest someone is a bit gullible and soft-hearted. These donations are just that—charity. House drakes avoid anything resembling a job, taking pride in their selfsufficiency and ability to choose how to spend their time.

([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 15

</column>

On a failed save, the creature is also [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.

**Silver Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The house drake breathes a 10-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of silver mist. Each creature within the mist must succeed at a DC 16 Will save or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 for 1 round. The house drake can't use Silver Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

**Silver Strike**   House drakes sharpen their jaws on silver ornamentation until they incorporate bits of silver in their teeth. Their jaws Strike counts as [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Ancient Tails</title>

Some of the oldest tales told among Varisian travelers speak of tiny dragons sent by [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281) to comfort and aid her followers during a long-forgotten tyranny. With the recent rediscovery of the ancient Thassilonian empire, some suggest that these tales—and thus house drakes themselves—first appeared back in that distant age.
</aside>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The aquatic, wingless offspring of dragonflies are called dragonfly nymphs. Giant dragonfly nymphs can be several feet in length and mostly hunt in shallow waters, eating carrion and ambushing living creatures with their fully grown counterparts. They're not daunted by larger creatures, compelled by their instinct to eat as much as possible to sustain their growth.

Drainberry bushes are floating bushes that originate from the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35), with long, thorny vines and dense clusters of bright-red berries. Their hollow thorns quickly siphon blood, which is how the carnivorous plants feed, and they rapidly turn consumed blood into fresh batches of delicious berries. They exude a faint white glow that's a result of stored vitality energy. Drainberry bushes exhibit unusually high intelligence and have an astute sense of value. When creatures attempt to harvest their berries, they typically become insulted by the lack of bartering. Creatures that attempt to converse with drainberry bushes find the plants telepathically convey only short and simple phrases: most commonly, “Money please,” “Deal good,” “Deal no good,” “Want that,” (with a gesture toward an item it covets), “Thank you, customer,” and if necessary, “No refunds.” Though a drainberry bush considers the market value of its berries to be 25 gp, it greatly prefers interesting art objects as payment—even ones of significantly lower value. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Drainberry Bush](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4373) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 139 **Perception** +16; [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 120 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); (can't speak any language), [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +13, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +11 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +6 **Int** -2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **Nature Empathy** The drainberry bush can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on and make very simple [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) of animals and plant creatures. --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +13 **Will** +13 **HP** 135 **Resistances** [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 10 **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5 --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** vine +17 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+10 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Blood Berries** The drainberry bush must drain blood from living creatures for sustenance. This causes clusters of bright red berries to grow among its branches. Each cluster of berries lasts for 1 day, and it typically has 1d6+3 clusters when encountered. When consumed, a cluster restores 2d8+10 Hit Points. This effect has the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) and [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) traits.
A creature can pluck a cluster of berries with a successful unarmed [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check against the bush's AC. **Consume Berries** ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) The bush draws nourishment from one cluster of blood berries, regaining 2d8+10 Hit Points. That berry cluster wrinkles and dies. **Drain Blood** **Requirements** The drainberry bush has at least one living creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) with one of its vines; **Effect** The bush squeezes all creatures it has grabbed, its hollow thorns piercing flesh and siphoning blood. Each creature must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or take 2d8+10 piercing damage and become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (double damage and drained 2 on a critical failure). For every creature damaged this way, a cluster of blood berries (see above) immediately grows along the bush's branches. **Storm of Vines** The drainberry bush makes up to four vine Strikes, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the bush's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the bush makes all these attacks.
Drainberry Collections As drainberry bushes sell their berries to others, they collect coins and small curios—such as a cameo depicting a fey noble, a lock of golden hair knotted in a complex pattern, or a ring inscribed “To my dearest Memdaria.” Not all of these baubles have monetary value, but those that don't certainly had emotional value to the original owner. Occasionally, a drainberry bush accepts intangible trade goods, such as odes celebrating the bush's grandeur.
name: Drainberry Bush

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Draugr](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4374)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The risen corpses of sailors who died at sea, known as draugrs, reek of rot and decay from the briny deep. Their eyes glow with ghastly green light, and rotting seaweed, barnacles, and dead sea creatures cling to their bodies. Draugrs can't speak, but they express their malicious emotions with gurgles, as though they were eternally drowning with lungs full of water. They desire little more than to attack living creatures, especially those who sail the sea. Even when they go upon land, draugrs often drag the corpses of those they strike down back to the water, populating the depths with even more dead.

Draugrs rise in the haunted places of the sea, where restless spirits, swells of void energy, or supernatural storms deliver death. A corpse might rest at the bottom of the sea for some time before awakening as a draugr. Collecting detritus and organisms, the body becomes increasingly disgusting before it finally rises. Proximity to intelligent life can expedite this process, and an underwater explorer who happens upon a shipwreck might cause a body to suddenly return to unlife as a draugr. These undead don't take intrusions lightly, especially upon their place of death.

Though hateful of the living, draugrs are susceptible to reminders of their lives as mariners. In particular, a well-performed sea shanty or call-andresponse work song might cause a draugr to become lost in reverie for a moment. The creatures have even been witnessed moaning along, unable to sing the words but providing haunting accompaniment. The lull rarely lasts long, though, as the beauty of the song quickly becomes a reminder of the tragedy that befell the draugr, reaffirming their desire for blood and death.

More powerful draugrs with burning red eyes are called draugr captains. They're 3rd-level creatures with the [elite adjustment](/Rules.aspx?ID=3264) that can cast [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606) as an [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) spell 3 times per day.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Draugr.png" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=722" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=732" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 140

**Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155); (can't speak any language)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +8

<row gap="medium">

**Dex** +2

**Con** +3

**Int** -1

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Items**
[Greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=376), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +11 

**Ref** +6 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 35
([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83))

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95)

**Resistances**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 3

**Weaknesses**
[vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) 5

**The Sea's Revenge** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A creature that slays a draugr is subjected to a mariner's curse spell with a DC of 17. The [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) ends if the draugr is buried in a calm sea or after 1 week passes.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, swim 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
greataxe +10 ([Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 1d12+4 slashing plus grotesque gift

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning plus grotesque gift

**Grotesque Gift**  ([Olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664)) A draugr's attacks spatter their targets with rancid flesh and rotting seaweed. A creature damaged by a draugr's Strike must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure).

**Swipe** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The draugr powers their hate into attacking as many foes as possible. The draugr makes a melee Strike and compares the attack roll result to the AC of up to two foes, each of whom must be within its melee reach and adjacent to each other. Roll damage only once and apply it to each creature hit. A Swipe counts as two attacks for the draugr's multiple attack penalty.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Draugr Ships</title>

When an entire ship's crew dies in one calamity, they might rise simultaneously, bound together in death. Tirelessly plaguing the seas, these draugr crews slowly corrupt their vessels. The cursed ships often exhibit unsettling phenomena, such as being able to sail against the wind or leaving schools of dead fish in their wake. Sea shanties tell of draugr raiders approaching during the night, wreathed in fog pierced by only the green glow emanating from their eyes.
</aside>
name: Draugr

Dweomercats are magically gifted, four-eyed felines from the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35), where they prey upon other creatures and feed on their primal energy. Within the First World, they form packs that hunt together, though they prefer to care for their cubs on their own, raising them on a diet of magic and magical creatures. Curious creatures, they're sometimes encountered in areas of [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) where the veil to the First World is thin or in regions where magic has been irreparably warped. Drawn there by the unique magical resonance, the dweomercats hope to acquire incomparable sources of food. Where dweomercats sighting have been reported, specialized trappers will appear soon after, hoping for the catch of a lifetime. A dweomercat's dark purple fur is prized by magical collectors, as the patterns that flow through the fur appear as though they were hand-painted, with spirals and curls resembling magical runes. These coats are then either displayed or included as components in rare rituals. Dweomercats are famous for their ability to twist the metaphorical strings of spells cast on or near them, which they can transform into their own defensive magic or use to instantly teleport across the battlefield. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Dweomercat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4375) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 141 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +16, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +15 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +17 **Will** +17 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 100 **Alter Dweomer** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Trigger** The dweomercat is targeted by a spell or is within the area of a spell as it's cast; **Effect** The dweomercat's runelike patterns start to glow as it gains an effect related to the tradition of the triggering spell. This effect occurs before the dweomercat is affected by the triggering spell. The effect lasts for 1 minute, until the dweomercat uses this ability again, or until the dweomercat [Dismisses](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the effect, whichever comes first.
**Arcane** Magical feedback deals 4d6 force damage to the triggering spellcaster (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
**Divine** The dweomercat gains a +1 status bonus to all skill checks.
**Occult** The dweomercat becomes [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83). This effect ends if the dweomercat uses a hostile action, in addition to the normal end conditions.
**Primal** The dweomercat gains 10 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321). --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** jaws +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d10+7 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+7 slashing **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 25 - **4th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (at will), [Dispelling Globe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (×3) - **Constant (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) **Dweomer Leap** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Prerequisites** The dweomercat has at least one [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) spell remaining; **Effect** The dweomercat casts _translocate_, then can make a melee Strike against one creature adjacent to it at the end of its teleport. If the dweomercat ends its teleport adjacent to a creature under an ongoing spell effect or who cast a spell since the dweomercat's last turn, this does not expend a casting of _translocate_. **Pounce** The dweomercat Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If the dweomercat began this action hidden, it remains hidden until after this ability's Strike.
Dweomercat Familiars Particularly powerful spellcasters sometimes take young dweomercat cubs as familiars. Their independence and flightiness make dweomercats somewhat unreliable allies, though, so one is never sure whether their bond with a dweomercat will be long-lasting or a temporary affair.
name: Dweomercat

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[D'ziriak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4376)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These strange creatures are native to the Netherworld, where their colorful nature is in opposition to that realm's overwhelmingly monochromatic palette. Averaging 7 feet in height, d'ziriaks have four arms, two legs, and a termite-like abdomen. The larger pair of arms, used for most tasks, have five-fingered hands with sharp, insectile claws. The smaller pair of arms are reserved for fine manipulations and are not effective in combat.

D'ziriaks' otherwise-dull brown carapaces are decorated with numerous runes glowing in bright colors. These tattoo-like runes indicate an individual's role in d'ziriak society and set them apart from their home plane's other native inhabitants. The runes glow with natural bioluminescence, and d'ziriaks can make them flare brightly for an instant, at the expense of overstressing the biochemical glands that create and maintain the runes for an extended time. The color and shape of the runes are partially natural but can be carefully customized over time to fit the individual's station.

The D'ziriak language is a mix of buzzes and chitters and is spoken by few other creatures. D'ziriaks prefer to communicate with other species using telepathy rather than endure the sound of their language being “butchered by fleshy throats.” D'ziriaks organize into hive cities led by a king and queen. These hive cities consist of impressive spires, yet the towers are only the foremost part of the settlement, with many chambers reaching deep below, used as residences, workshops, and fungus farms. D'ziriak settlements are lit inside and out with alchemical and magical light sources, often in the shape of runes. These dimly glowing towers provide travelers with landmarks and perhaps promise safe havens on the otherwise gloomy [Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36).

<column gap="tiny">

**Glow** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) 20 feet. The colorful runes that decorate a d'ziriak's body create [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403). The natural bioluminescence is specially adapted to [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36), able to overcome magical darkness as if it were magical light with a rank equal to half the d'ziriak's level rounded up.

</column>

**Melee**

**Damage** 1d10+4 piercing

- **7th**
[Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (self only; to [Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) only)

**Dazzling Burst** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The d'ziriak causes their body to flare with intense colorful light. Non-d'ziriaks in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save. After using this ability during this time they can't use Dazzling Burst again. A creature that attempts this save is immune to all Dazzling Bursts for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected. **Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round. **Failure** The creature is dazzled for 1 minute. **Critical Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round and dazzled for 1 minute.

**Double Claw** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The d'ziriak makes two claw Strikes. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses. This counts as two attacks for the d'ziriak's multiple attack penalty, and the penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Light Weavers</title>

The masters of d'ziriak light-weaving craft are occult practitioners, almost always [sorcerers](/Classes.aspx?ID=62) of the [aberrant](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=19) bloodline. Light weavers prefer spells that provide light or create magical writing. In their hive cities, light weavers create art, lighting, signage, and magic wards. They also use their light weaving for entertainment and education.
</aside>
name: D'ziriak

markdown:
Elasmosauruses are long-necked, primeval reptiles that dwell in deep oceans and seas. Although not truly a dinosaur, elasmosauruses are often found in similar locations and are similarly titanic creatures. Keeping their massive bodies underwater, elasmosauruses use their long necks to catch prey and snorkel air to their massive lungs while remaining mostly hidden from the surface above. An elasmosaurus is 30 feet long and weighs 6,000 pounds. As rare and reclusive as they are, elasmosauruses are sometimes mistaken for even rarer creatures called water orms, legendary aquatic denizens of remote lakes known for their elusiveness and craftiness. Whereas elasmosauruses are mundane creatures of animalistic intelligence, water orms are magical beings with near-humanoid intelligence and a curious fascination with mortals, and they seem to delight in confounding onlookers. As a result, it's theorized that a typical water orm is more than happy to lead a group of spectators to a lost elasmosaurus, both to throw its pursuers off its tail and for the hilarity that will inevitably ensue. While elasmosauruses are often found in lost worlds and unsettled regions, those who dwell in the oceans of the world do not contain their hunting grounds to specific regions. As a result, it's not unheard of for a wandering specimen to find its way to coastal waters. Those that do often find the ports of small towns or even large cities to be wondrous banquets and are usually hunted down by coastal guards or adventurers. When a wayward elasmosaurus like this finds its way into a city's sewer system or reservoirs, though, it can become the stuff of urban legends. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Elasmosaurus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4377) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 143 **Perception** +16; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** -1 Deep Breath The elasmosaurus can hold its breath for 2 hours. --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +13 **Will** +16 **HP** 125 **Long Neck** An elasmosaurus's long neck allows it to interact with the surface while its body remains submerged underwater. While submerged no deeper than 15 feet underwater, an elasmosaurus can still stick its head up to breathe. An elasmosaurus gains [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) against attacks made against creatures that are above the water's surface while it is underwater, even if its head is above the surface.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** Jaws only. --- **Speed** 5 feet, swim 35 feet **Melee** jaws +16 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+10 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** paddle +16, **Damage** 2d6+10 bludgeoning **Drag Below** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) The elasmosaurus attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against a [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) foe's Fortitude DC. If the elasmosaurus succeeds, the foe is forcibly moved 5 feet toward the elasmosaurus's body. If the elasmosaurus critically succeeds, the foe is moved 10 feet toward the elasmosaurus's body. **Thrashing Retreat** A swimming elasmosaurus thrashes the area around it as it attempts to flee. It makes two paddle Strikes, each of which must be against separate targets, and each of which takes the normal multiple attack penalty. It then [Swims](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) up to its swim Speed. This Swim does not trigger reactions based on movement.
Other Aquatic Reptiles The elasmosaurus is but one of many types of aquatic reptiles found in primeval oceans or lost worlds. Some, like the ichthyosaurus, are almost fishlike in appearance. Others, such as the mosasaurus, are truly massive beasts capable of killing and eating whales.
name: Elasmosaurus
