<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Smoke Creeper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4380)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Smoke creepers shift and modify their appearance as they float through the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=29), but they prefer to take on a vaguely insectile form with wings trailing choking fumes and glowing red eyes.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Smoke Creeper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4380)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=527" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=587" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 144

**Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), smoke vision

**Languages**
[Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +5

**Con** +3

**Int** -2

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Smoke Vision** The smoke creeper ignores the concealed condition from smoke.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +13 

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 80

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

**Resistances**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5

**Smoke Form** The smoke creeper can move through the spaces of other creatures but can't end its movement in the same space.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
smoke mandibles +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 2d8+3 piercing plus 1d6 poison

**Choking Swoop** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The smoke creeper [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to its Speed, moving through the spaces of other creatures and leaving traces of itself behind. Each breathing creature it passes through must attempt a DC 23 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature inhales part of the elemental and is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for 1 minute by the pain of the smoke rasping in its throat and lungs. A creature can attempt to end this condition by spending an action coughing and succeeding at a DC 23 Fortitude save.

**Painful Exhalations** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** At least one creature within 40 feet is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) from the smoke creeper's Choking Swoop; **Effect** The smoke creeper flaps its wings, violently drawing the lingering smoke free from all creatures immobilized from its Choking Swoop within 40 feet. Each target must attempt a DC 23 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute and [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (enfeebled 2 and sickened 2 on a critical failure). Regardless of the result, the creature is no longer immobilized from the smoke creeper's Choking Swoop.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Air And Smoke</title>

Elementals of smoke, such as the smoke creeper, are often cruel in their attacks on breathing creatures, causing them to choke and cough from their noxious fumes. Some believe that they hold secret allegiances to [Ymeri](/Deities.aspx?ID=697), who holds domain over fire and smoke.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-408" />
name: Smoke Creeper

While the melody on the wind, known by some as a song elemental, might enjoy the beauty of music, it is by nature a destructive elemental force. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Melody On The Wind](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4381) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 145 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +22 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +22 **Will** +19 **HP** 170 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Hostile Duet** **Trigger** A hostile creature within 30 feet creates an effect with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait that provides bonuses to itself or its allies; **Effect** The melody on the wind recreates the effect, gaining the bonuses for itself and its allies as long as the original effect persists.
**Retune** **Trigger** The melody on the wind is targeted by a spell that has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait; **Effect** The melody on the wind attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the spell with a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check. If it succeeds, the spell effect is caught in a blast of wind that sweeps it back to its origin, affecting the caster. Targets of the triggering effect other than the melody on the wind are still affected normally. --- **Speed** fly 100 feet; swiftness **Melee** wind gust +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d10+10 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) **Ranged** solid refrain +23 ([range increment 70 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d8+10 sonic **Mesmerizing Melody** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The melody on the wind sings in a sonorous chorus. Any creature in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 29 Will save to resist becoming [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by the melody on the wind. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is fascinated by the melody on the wind for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is fascinated by the melody on the wind for 4 rounds. **Swiftness** The melody on the wind's movement doesn't trigger reactions.
name: Melody On The Wind

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sand Sentry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4384)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

This creature of pure sand moves with an eerie grace, shifting back and forth between a detailed likeness of a human and a muted and featureless bipedal form. The sand sentry is often called upon by spellcasters to stand guard over an area of great importance; these elementals are patient participants in such roles, making them well suited for long-term service.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Sand Sentry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4384)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=585" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=587" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 146

**Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +1
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +16 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 90

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

**Glass Armor** When the sand sentry takes electricity or fire damage, its outer layer of sand fuses into sheets of hardened glass for 1 minute. This increases the sand sentry's AC to 26 and grants it resistance 5 to [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), piercing, and slashing damage. A sand sentry can't use earth glide while glass armor is active.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 50 feet; earth glide

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +17,
**Damage** 2d8+8 bludgeoning plus blinding sand

**Earth Glide**   A sand sentry can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through earthen matter, including rock. When it does so, it moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.

**Blinding Sand**   When the sand sentry critically hits with a fist Strike, the target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Sand Simulacra</title>

Sand sentries have no true culture or society of their own, but they're endlessly fascinated with the society and culture of humanoids they encounter. They can shape their appearance to mimic any similarly sized humanoids, and although they always remain obviously composed of sand, they do their best to copy the day-to-day activities they observe other humanoids performing. Of course, structures and objects sand sentries build from sand never last, but this never seems to stifle their obsession.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-409" />
name: Sand Sentry

This squat and stony creature looks like an armadillo with a large raised back and a flail-like tail. The granite glyptodont has no true need to feed, as with all elementals, yet it does seem to linger after transforming the flesh of those it calcifies, as if the mere proximity of flesh fossilizing into stone pleases it somehow. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Granite Glyptodont](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4385) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 147 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 90 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +1 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +13 **Will** +17 **HP** 145 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 20 feet; earth glide **Melee** tail +20 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d12+9 bludgeoning plus calcification **Calcification** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A blow from a granite glyptodont's tail Strike hardens the flesh of the creature struck. The target must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save or become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (slowed 2 on a critical failure). Further failed saves against calcification increase the value of the slowed condition. Once a creature's actions are reduced to 0 by calcification, that creature becomes [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87). If the creature isn't petrified, the slowed conditions end once 1 minute passes without the creature failing a save against calcification.
Every 24 hours after it was petrified, the creature can attempt a DC 26 Fortitude save to recover. On a success, it becomes flesh again but is slowed 1 for the next 24 hours. On a critical success, the creature recovers and isn't slowed. On a failure, the creature remains petrified but can try again in 24 hours. On a critical failure, the petrification is permanent, and the creature can't attempt any more saves. **Earth Glide** A granite glyptodont can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through earthen matter, including rock. When it does so, it moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.
name: Granite Glyptodont

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ember Fox](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4386)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ember foxes resemble their canid namesakes, save for the flames that make their fur and the tips of their long whiskers flicker and glow. They particularly enjoy hunting elementals from the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25).

**Cloak in Embers [reaction** ] **Trigger** An adjacent ally is targeted by an effect that deals fire damage; **Effect** The ember fox drapes itself across its ally, granting the ally fire [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 10 against the incoming attack.

These creatures look like roiling clouds of black smoke churning above a mound of burning refuse. Leering faces form and fade in the smoke above, while the burning detritus writhes in spasmodic lurches, obviously alive. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Filth Fire](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4387) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 148 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), smoke vision **Languages** [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9 **Str** +1 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 Smoke Vision The filth fire ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +13 **Will** +9 **HP** 70 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), sleep **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 5 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** burning lash +13 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d6+3 bludgeoning plus 1d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Ranged** ember ball +13 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+3 bludgeoning plus 1d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Noxious Burst** Toxic materials and churning rubbish within the filth fire's body explode in one of three ways. The filth fire chooses the effect, but it can't make the same choice twice in a row.
  • **Fiery Beam** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The filth fire expels a 30-foot line of flame that deals 3d6 fire damage with a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save.
  • **Shrapnel Blast** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The filth fire shoots jagged rubbish out in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) that deals 2d12 piercing damage with a DC 21 basic Reflex save.
  • **Toxic Fumes** ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The filth fire belches a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of toxic smoke that deals 2d6 poison damage (DC 21 basic Fortitude save). A creature that fails is also [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure).
name: Filth Fire

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

An embodiment of the speed and chaos of a spreading blaze, a striding fire appears as a lithe and long-limbed humanoid composed of shifting-hued flames churning around a skeleton-like framework.

<column gap="tiny">

([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22

<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 149

**Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), smoke vision

**Languages**
[Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110)

**Skills**

**Int** +0

**Cha** +1
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 115

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

**Weaknesses**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 2d8+5 bludgeoning plus 1d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86)

**Burning Rush** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The striding fire stretches out its legs to an impossible length, propelling it forward. The striding fire Strides up to double its Speed in a straight [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388). Its movement during this Stride doesn't trigger reactions. Any creature the striding fire was adjacent to at any point during this Stride must attempt a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. If it critically fails, it's knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) by a wave of heated air. The striding fire can't use Burning Rush for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-410" />
name: Striding Fire

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Icicle Snake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4390)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Translucent and capable of hanging suspended and near motionless, icicle snakes sense the heat of living creatures as a threat and attempt to use their camouflage and chilling bite against foes.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**

The tentacled mist stalker shrouds itself in a cloak of mist through which its single, never-blinking eye can see with clarity, allowing it an advantage when stalking prey. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Mist Stalker](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4391) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 150 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), mist vision **Languages** [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 **Mist Cloud** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) 15 feet. The mist stalker is surrounded by mist. Creatures in the aura are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62). If wind disperses the aura, it returns automatically at the start of the mist stalker's turn. This cloud is suppressed in water.
**Mist Vision** The mist stalker ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from mist and fog. --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +12 **Will** +11 **HP** 60 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) --- **Speed** 20 feet, climb 20 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** tentacle +14 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+4 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 1d8+4 bludgeoning, DC 21 **Solidify Mist** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The mist stalker makes its mist cloud congeal, causing the aura to be [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) until the start of the mist stalker's next turn. In addition, the mist stalker can make the mist even thicker around a single Medium or smaller creature within the cloud. The creature must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or become [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) or is no longer in the mist cloud's emanation.
name: Mist Stalker

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Blizzardborn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4392)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Freezing and thawing in a near-constant loop, blizzardborn resemble humanoid forms composed of a mixture of partially melted snow and sleet. These elementals move with crunching strides, their bodies constantly sloshing and sloughing off shards of ice. Because of their ability to refreeze, blizzardborn can travel into warmer environments safely, though they tend to look more like slush in these areas.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=555" />
<trait label="Elemental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=587" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=732" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 151

**Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), snow vision

**Languages**
[Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +14

<row gap="medium">

**Con** +4

**Int** +0

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Snow Vision** The blizzardborn ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from falling snow.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +16 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 105

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5

**Shattering Ice** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** An enemy hits the blizzardborn with an attack that deals physical damage; **Effect** A portion of the blizzardborn's body shatters into an explosion of razor-sharp ice crystals and blinding snow that deals 2d6 piercing damage to opponents in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Anyone who fails is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round (3 rounds on a critical failure).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, ice burrow 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ice claw +17 ([versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 2d6+8 slashing plus 1d6 [persistent cold](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86)

**Ice Burrow**   The blizzardborn can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through ice or snow with a Speed of 20 feet. It moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Icewyrm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4393)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Resembling wingless, serpentine dragons formed of jagged ice and shot through with veins of nearly frozen water, these elementals dwell within icebergs and enjoy striking out at passing ships or creatures. They're especially common in frigid stretches of ocean in the [Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=32), where icebergs cluster together into enormous islands of ice.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 27

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22
</column>

</column>

<traits>

<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=529" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=555" />

</traits>

**Skills**

---

**AC** 30 

**Fort** +20 

**Ref** +21 

**Will** +17 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 185

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10

**Explosion** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555)) When the icewyrm dies, it explodes, dealing 8d6 cold damage to each creature in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, ice burrow 20 feet, swim 60 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d6+13 slashing plus 1d6 [persistent cold](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ice shard +23 ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d6+13 piercing plus 1d6 [persistent cold](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86)

**Breathe Ice Shards** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The icewyrm breathes a 60-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) of freezing shards of razor-sharp ice, dealing 3d12 cold damage and 3d12 piercing damage to every creature in the line (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The icewyrm can't use Breathe Ice Shards again for 1d4 rounds.

**Ice Burrow**   The icewyrm can Burrow through ice or snow with a Speed of 20 feet. It moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.

</column>

Air wisps are floating spheres of cloud and storm, perpetually humming a light, whispery tone. They're playful and capricious, with great curiosity toward strangers. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Air Wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4394) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 152 **Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Plane of Air Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +7 **Str** +0 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +3 **Ref** +9 **Will** +4 **HP** 12 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resonance** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527)) 30 feet. All wisps vibrate at a frequency attuned to their element, resonating with and empowering all creatures and effects sharing that trait. Creatures in the area gain a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls for effects that have the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527) trait; a creature with the [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=587) and air traits gains this bonus to all attack and damage rolls.
**Accord Essence** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527)) **Trigger** An ally within 30 feet that benefited from the wisp's resonance in the last hour is targeted by an attack; **Effect** The wisp detonates themself in an elemental explosion. This grants [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to half the wisp's current Hit Points to allies within 30 feet who have benefited from the wisp's resonance in the last hour. These temporary Hit Points last 1 hour. A wisp that uses this reaction is permanently destroyed, and they can be restored by only a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual or similarly powerful effect. If an ability would prevent the wisp's destruction (for instance, if the wisp is summoned and would merely be dismissed), Accord Essence has no effect. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Melee** tendril +7 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning **In Concert** When an air wisp rolls a critical failure on a check to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292), they get a failure instead, and when they roll a success, they get a critical success instead.
name: Air Wisp

They're timid and aloof but fiercely loyal to those they know and trust. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Earth Wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4395) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 152 **Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +6, [Dungeon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +4, [Plane of Earth Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +4 **Str** +2 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +5 **Will** +4 **HP** 15 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** bludgeoning 2 **Resonance** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) 30 feet. All wisps vibrate at a frequency attuned to their element, resonating with and empowering all creatures and effects sharing that trait. Creatures in the area gain a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls for effects that have the [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585) trait; a creature with the [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=587) and earth traits gains this bonus to all attack and damage rolls.
**Accord Essence** ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) **Trigger** An ally within 30 feet that benefited from the wisp's resonance in the last hour is targeted by an attack; **Effect** The wisp detonates themself in an elemental explosion. This grants [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to half the wisp's current Hit Points to allies within 30 feet who have benefited from the wisp's resonance in the last hour. These temporary Hit Points last 1 hour. A wisp that uses this reaction is permanently destroyed, and they can be restored by only a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual or similarly powerful effect. If an ability would prevent the wisp's destruction (for instance, if the wisp is summoned and would merely be [dismissed](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311)), Accord Essence has no effect. --- **Speed** 20 feet, burrow 20 feet **Melee** tendril +6 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning **In Concert** When an earth wisp rolls a critical failure on a check to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292), they get a failure instead, and when they roll a success, they get a critical success instead.

Fire wisps are sizzling spheres of flame and ash, perpetually crackling and shimmering with heat. **Accord Essence** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Trigger** An ally within 30 feet that benefited from the wisp's resonance in the last hour is targeted by an attack; **Effect** The wisp detonates themself in an elemental explosion. This grants [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to half the wisp's current Hit Points to allies within 30 feet who have benefited from the wisp's resonance in the last hour. These temporary Hit Points last 1 hour. A wisp that uses this reaction is permanently destroyed, and they can be restored by only a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual or similarly powerful effect. If an ability would prevent the wisp's destruction (for instance, if the wisp is summoned and would merely be [dismissed](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311)), Accord Essence has no effect. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 15 feet **Melee** tendril +6 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d4+1 fire plus 1 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **In Concert** When a fire wisp rolls a critical failure on a check to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292), they get a failure instead, and when they roll a success, they get a critical success instead.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ether Spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4398)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ether spiders are members of a vague taxonomy called ethereal wildlife and are deadly predators from the Ethereal Plane. They are giant arachnids who can shape the raw essence of the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) as easily as spinning silk, weaving it in complex patterns that drift through the misty void. From these ethereal nests, whole families of ether spiders can scout adjacent locales in the Universe, watching for easy prey in dark or remote corners of the land of mortals. Once an ether spider has spotted a meal, it anchors its nest and waits on the Ethereal Plane for its prey to draw near. As soon as its victim is within reach, the ether spider shifts to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), clamps its fangs onto its prey, then shifts back to the Ethereal Plane to wait as its venom works through the creature's system. Ether spiders move between the planes with ease, making them extremely dangerous to those who can't see or attack ethereal enemies. 

Ether spiders are not mindless or cruel—they are simply hungry. If a prospective meal can sate an ether spider's incredible appetite through other means, they might be able to bargain for their life. Ether spiders are especially interested in items, information, or allies who can help them against their enemies. 

Ether spiders dwell in vast nests adrift in the Ethereal Plane, where as many as half a dozen ether spiders might cohabitate. Although ether spiders enjoy one another's company, they don't form the same bonds as most humanoids, and they are more likely to feast on the corpse of a fallen sibling than save one from certain doom. When not hunting prey, ether spiders are likely to let down their guard. Travelers who find a floating ether spider commune should have an easier time of making peaceful contact to trade with or even befriend these strange arachnids. The nests drift on metaphysical currents and are rarely seen in the same vicinity twice.

Sometimes a point of interest in the neighboring Universe compels them to tether their nest to an area, creating a semipermanent home.

<actions string="Single Action" />

**Ether Spider Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage, clumsy 2 and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 3d6 poison damage, clumsy 3 and slowed 2 (1 round)

**Ethereal Step** <actions string="Single Action" />  The ether spider shifts to either the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) or [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). The ether spider can remain on the Ethereal Plane indefinitely without ill effect. While there, it can see clearly into the Universe with a range of 60 feet. On its first round in an encounter, the ether spider can use this ability once as a free action.

**Ethereal Web Trap**   A creature hit by the ether spider's web attack is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) and stuck to the nearest surface ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 22).

**Web Burst** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The ether spider flings a gout of stored webs in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). These webs can pass between [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) and the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34). Each creature in the area is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), as ethereal web trap, unless it succeeds at a DC 22 Reflex save.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Ether Spider Foes</title>

The [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) is a sparsely populated realm in comparison to most, used more for traveling than dwelling. Yet the deep mists here conceal denizens such as monstrous parasitic beings that use humanoids as incubators for eggs, [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) born from the raw fears of mortal life, and ether spiders that are ever vigilant against those foes' machinations.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Festrog](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4399)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Spawned from corpses of those who died of illness or starvation and were twisted by void energy, festrogs display a viciousness that rivals other undead. They resemble decaying humanoids, but with elongated arms, teeth, and bone-like spikes protruding from their upper backs. Festrogs' tendency to run on all fours has led to them gaining the moniker dog-ghouls, causing the unwary to mistake them for mindless predators.

Festrogs are in fact intelligent, stalking victims in packs and choosing hunting grounds that suit their abilities. They can often be found roaming farmlands, open forests, or wide plains, anywhere they can use their quadrupedal speed to overtake quarry. Belying their mindless appearance, festrogs use tactics similar to those of hunters with hounds: the leader of the pack often flushes prey from cover so that the victim can be brought down by the pack.

Researchers of the necromantic processes that create risen corpses have found that festrogs spawn more often from slow demises than sudden deaths. Festrogs typically animate from corpses afflicted with disease, while victims of violence are more likely to become more common undead, such as zombies. One well-documented way to create a festrog is to have scavengers feed on the dead flesh before animating it. This potentially explains why festrogs most often arise in remote areas stricken with famine and desperate predators.

Though most festrogs arise from humans and other prevalent humanoids, other creatures that die in the same circumstances can rise as festrogs. Festrogs that were once larger humanoids, such as [ogres](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=451), [hill giants](/Monsters.aspx?ID=220), or [trolls](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=478) behave similarly to other humanoid festrogs and are simply larger and more powerful. More peculiar are the [beastkin](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=29) festrogs formed of quadrupedal animals, which typically have feet or hooves instead of hands, travel on all fours at all times, and have only animal intelligence. In fact, some humanoid festrogs capture and starve beastkin in hopes of the creatures rising as grotesque festrog pets.

These temporary HP last for 1 minute.

**On All Fours** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The festrog has nothing in their hands; **Effect** The festrog Strides with a +10-foot circumstance bonus to their Speed.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Whispering Hounds</title>

Since the fall of Lastwall and the creation of the Gravelands, festrogs have become more common. They prowl the edges of once-peaceful farmlands and often precede a horde of further horrors. Rumors have begun to circulate that the necromancers of the Whispering Way use these creatures as advance scouts to find vulnerable towns and sow terror. As a result, attacks that once would have been dismissed as the predation of wolf packs now evoke a much greater degree of panic.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Fly](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4402)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

This massive fly's twitching limbs seem to never stop moving as it constantly cleans itself and scoops anything edible into its mouthparts. While primarily carrion eaters, giant flies sometimes prey on livestock or other animals too slow to get away. They are not particularly dangerous, and are mostly reviled due to their obnoxious droning and habit of spreading disease.

markdown:
Garudas are winged humanoids created to serve [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) gods and other powerful celestial beings. Originally born among the celestial planes, garudas eventually spread beyond, with many making their way to the mortal Universe. As divine servants, garudas' primary roles are as protectors, defending holy sites and important divine figures like high priests. The most important role a garuda serves is as a means of transportation. The first garudas served as mounts for the gods, carrying them across the heavens and into combat. This tradition continues today, with garudas willingly serving as mounts for champions, clerics, and other divine chosen. On occasion, a god will request a garuda track down one of the god's followers. The garuda declares their servitude to the follower, acting as a guardian and transport for as long as the god decrees. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Garuda](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4405) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 159 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Vudrani](/Languages.aspx?ID=175); plus two others; speaker of the skies **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +19 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 **Speaker of the Skies** A garuda can speak with any type of bird.
**Vehicle of the Gods** Garudas were created to serve as transport for other beings. A [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) creature can ride the garuda by using the [Mount](/Actions.aspx?ID=2314) action to move onto them. Unlike the normal rules for riding other creatures, both the garuda and the rider continue to receive all 3 of their actions each turn, and the garuda's rider can be Medium or smaller. A ga ruda can have only a single rider at a time. The garuda can choose to allow a non-holy (but not an [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) creature to ride them, but generally only does so in specific circumstances. --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +19 **Will** +16 **HP** 160 **Resistances** 10 **Weaknesses** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 10 --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** beak +21 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d10+9 piercing plus 1d6 fire **Melee** talon +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning plus 1d6 fire and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Ranged** sun beam +19 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d10 fire plus 2d6 spirit **Divine Innate Spells** DC 25, attack +18 - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553) - **4th** [Blazing Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450), [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) **Celestial Meteor** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) **Requirements** The garuda doesn't have a rider; **Effect** The garuda [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) straight up and then comes crashing down toward the ground, landing in an unoccupied space within 30 feet. As the garuda lands, a burst of solar flames erupts from them, dealing 5d6 fire damage to all creatures in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). If the garuda lands adjacent to a creature, they can attempt to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) that creature. On a success or critical success, the garuda can then Fly up to 30 feet with the creature. **Divine Grasp** When a garuda moves, they can bring [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) creatures along with them. **Raise by the Sun** **Requirements** The garuda doesn't have a rider; **Effect** The garuda [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) and picks up a willing creature at any point during the flight, who then begins riding the garuda, and then the garuda continues their Fly action. If the garuda uses three actions, they can instead Fly twice. At any point during the garuda's movement, the rider can use a reaction to attempt a Strike with a ranged weapon.
Sun-Borne Servants According to the old tales, the first garudas were born from celestial eggs kept warm within the heart of the sun. These eggs incubated for hundreds of years. Most of the eggs hatched at different times, revealing a number of different celestial creatures, but the last clutch of eggs remained, waiting patiently until ordered to open. Once one hundred and eight lifetimes passed, the gods returned to the sun and bid the garudas be born. Seeing as they were obedient even before their birth, the gods made garudas their blessed servants.
name: Garuda

markdown:
Hungry ghosts arise from those who didn't receive proper burials or whose graves were neglected. They are not bound to a site or item but are compelled to see opportunities to commit good deeds in hopes of gaining favors that can aid them in achieving a final rest. Their need to feed on living energy often conflicts with this goal, however. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 22 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Hungry Ghost](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4406) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 160 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165); one other language **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +14, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +14, [Ghost Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +18, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +15 **Str** -5 **Dex** +5 **Con** +0 **Int** +4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Living Visage** While they have more than 30 HP, the hungry ghost appears to be a living creature. They have an automatic result of 34 on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks and DCs to conceal their undead status and can Feed on the Living covertly (below).
**Ravenous Undoing** In each 24-hour period, the hungry ghost must use Feed on the Living to consume 30 HP (any HP the ghost would gain count toward this total, even if the ghost has enough HP that they don't actually regain the full amount). If the ghost hasn't consumed enough HP, they mindlessly and recklessly feed on any living creature they come across until satiated. --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +15 **Will** +15 **HP** 60 (rejuvenation, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** all damage 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-magical) **Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When a hungry ghost is destroyed, they reform after 2d4 days fully healed at the location where they were last destroyed. They're only permanently destroyed when they have been given a proper burial, have had their grave cleaned and maintained for at least a year, or have been judged to be redeemed by [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290). --- **Speed** fly 25 feet **Melee** ghostly touch +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 2d8+4 void **Feed on the Living** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The hungry ghost touches a living creature in reach to steal its life force. If the ghost is in their living visage, they can disguise Feed on the Living as a benign touch and delay the effects for up to 1 minute while keeping the target unaware of the effect. A creature can be affected by only one delayed Feed on the Living at a time, and if the ghost loses their living visage during that minute, the delayed effect is lost. When Feed on the Living takes effect, the target takes 2d8+4 void damage, depending on the result of its DC 24 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target's life energy overpowers the ghost. The hungry ghost takes 5 vitality damage, and the target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage, and the hungry ghost regains HP equal to the damage dealt.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute, and the hungry ghost regains HP equal to the damage dealt.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage and is enfeebled 2 for 1 minute, and the hungry ghost regains HP equal to the damage dealt.
Unremembered, Unmourned A ghost is driven by an all-consuming need, connected to their death but often unclear to the ghost themself. Fragments of their previous life still bind to the ghost's identity, but they become shrouded or twisted in service of the ghost's death energy. They pull the ghost toward committing horrific and vengeful acts, like invisible marionette strings clutched by a sinister force. This disconnect means the ghost is rarely helpful to those who hope to set it to rest, forcing them to seek out clues from the environment or other creatures' recollections. Even a hungry ghost, compelled to commit good deeds, is helpless to keep control against their need to feed.
name: Hungry Ghost

creature_ability: Clutching Hands   Frequency  once per round
markdown:
When a horrific tragedy results in mass death, the restless spirits of the numerous dead might arise as a ghostly mob. Like other ghosts, ghostly mobs are often unaware they're dead. The spirits trapped within the mob can try to carry out a semblance of their former lives, even though their memories are fragmentary and their forms are insubstantial. This fragmented memory revolves around their last moments in that location, which compels the ghosts to instinctually play out these last moments, sometimes ranging from a few minutes to entire days' worth of time. When one or more of the members of a ghostly mob are forced to confront their undead nature, the spirits lash out in pain and violence. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Ghostly Mob](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4407) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 161 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Dwelling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18 **Str** -5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 **Site Bound** A ghostly mob can stray no farther than 240 feet from where its members were killed. --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +14 **Will** +18 **HP** 105 (4 segments, rejuvenation, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-magical) **Weaknesses** area damage 8, splash damage 8 **Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When a ghostly mob is destroyed, it reforms after 2d4 days within the location it's bound to, fully healed. A ghostly mob can be permanently destroyed only if someone sets right whatever prevents the troop from resting.
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86)** --- **Speed** fly 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Clutching Hands **Frequency** once per round** **Effect** The troop attacks enemies in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), with a DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6+3 void damage
3d6+6 void damage
4d6+9 void damage **Frightful Chorus** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The ghostly mob howls in anguish, sharing the pain of their death with any living creature that can hear them. This painful wailing forces each living creature in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) to attempt a DC 26 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 (frightened 3 on a critical failure). Regardless of the save result, the creature is then temporarily immune to the troop's Frightful Chorus for 1 minute.
Echoes Of Tragedy The specific tragedy that created a ghostly mob might alter its abilities. For example, a ghostly mob spawned from a fire might have Burning Grasp rather than Clutching Hands, which deals fire damage instead of void damage. A ghostly mob created in an earthquake might have Earthshaking Chorus rather than Frightful Chorus, which causes living victims to be [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 1 round rather than [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76).
name: Ghostly Mob

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Cave Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4408)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Cave giants are the vicious loners of the giant world. Even other giantkin mostly find cave giants too brutal and antisocial to form alliances with. For their part, cave giants—garbed in stinking hides festooned with the rotting skulls of their victims—seem ambivalent about their foul reputation.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Giant_CaveGiant.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Cave Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4408)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Giant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=616" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 162

**Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +3

**Con** +5

**Int** -2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Items**
_[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [ greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=376)_, [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 23 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 110

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
greataxe +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 1d12+9 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d8+9 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rock +16 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d6+10 bludgeoning

**Forceful Fracture** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The cave giant is within reach of any kind of stone; **Effect** The cave giant pounds their fist into the nearby stone, fracturing it into chunks to hurl at other creatures. The giant then makes two ranged rock Strikes, each targeting a separate creature. Both attacks count toward their multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after they've made both attacks.

**Smear** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Requirements** The cave giant is within reach of a creature that is adjacent to a wall or other solid vertical surface; **Effect** The cave giant snags the creature and smashes it against the wall. The giant attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the target's Reflex DC. On a success, the cave giant [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the creature and smears it along the nearby wall, dealing 2d8+8 bludgeoning damage. On a critical success, the damage is doubled.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Cave Giant Allies</title>

A lucky cave giant might manage to capture and tame a giant reptile, such as a monitor lizard or giant salamander, to serve as a maltreated pet or guard beast. These reptiles are often abused by their cruel keepers, learning to react toward all humanoids with fear and violence.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-420" />

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Desert Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4409)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Desert giants are nomadic humanoids who have dwelled in the world's most arid regions since time immemorial. Smaller peoples know that desert giants are the undisputed masters of desert living. Desert giants' strong cultural traditions play a large part in their ability to prosper in such harsh environs. Desert giant elders encourage their descendants to maintain abstemious lifestyles, particularly with regard to the consumption of food and drink. Most desert giants follow a simple vegetarian diet and maintain an incredible internal map of their home region's seasonal waterways and oases. 

Desert giants stand 15 feet tall, tending toward lean physiques that are ideal for traveling across vast expanses of sand for long sojourns.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Giant_DesertGiant.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 9">[Desert Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4409)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Giant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=616" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 162

**Perception** +19

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +21, [Desert Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +19

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +6

**Con** +5

**Int** +3

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Doubling Rings](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3079), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41), [Scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=393), _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=393)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 27 

**Fort** +18 

**Ref** +21 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 185

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_scimitar_ +21 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 2d6+12 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rock +19 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d8+12 bludgeoning

**Sand Spin** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The desert giant is standing in sandy terrain; **Effect** The desert giant spins around and stirs up loose sand in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Until the beginning of the giant's next turn, creatures in the area are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and other creatures are concealed to them.

**Sandwalking**   Desert giants have adapted to the loose sands of the desert and can move across them with ease. Desert giants ignore non-magical [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2370) caused by sand.

**Scimitar Blitz** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The desert giant [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to their Speed, Striking once with each of their scimitars at any point during the movement.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Oasis Protectors</title>

Keenly aware of their native environs' fragile ecosystem, desert giants fiercely protect their favorite watering holes and ancestral oases from newcomers who might despoil the local flora or chase away scarce fauna. Anyone who convinces them they mean no harm is welcomed, and there are tales of them aiding those hurt by the harshness of the desert.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-420" />
name: Desert Giant

markdown:
Rugged and covered in pustules and sores from wandering harsh wastelands, ash giants dwell in the unwelcoming wilderness. Their lives are difficult, hardening them into utterly vicious, capricious, and cruel creatures. Their sadistic sense of humor and love for pranks makes them hated by almost all humanoids they encounter. Despite being crass and rough, ash giants are survivors above all, and they use their ingenuity to craft weapons, traps, harnesses for mounts, and tools. If the aim is cruelty, brutality, or a prank, an ash giant will find a way to craft the tools they need. Piggy clods are a favorite construction— contraptions of scrap iron and other metals rigged into a ball that explodes on impact. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Ash Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4410) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 163 **Perception** +21; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +24, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +16, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +21, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +21 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +6 **Int** -1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** -2 **Vermin Empathy** The ash giant can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the Diplomacy skill with insects, arachnids, and similar creatures. **Items** [piggy clod](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1688) (6), _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ war flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=402)_ --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +18 **Will** +21 +2 status to all saves vs. [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **HP** 240 **Tumor Pop** When the ash giant takes piercing damage while they have a swollen tumor, the tumor explodes automatically, with the effect of Blastboil.
**[Reactive Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2256)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** war flail +25 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 2d10+13 bludgeoning plus Gore Grinder **Melee** fist +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 2d4+13 bludgeoning **Ranged** piggy clod +24 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [thrown 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 2d8+7 slashing plus 5 slashing [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage **Blastboil** The ash giant pops one of the massive, swollen pustules on their body. Each creature in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) takes 5d8 poison damage with a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. A creature that fails its save is also [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (or sickened 2 on a critical failure). This ability and tumor pop can't be used again until another tumor swells to a suitable size in 1d4 rounds. **Gore Grinder** **Requirements** The ash giant's last action was a successful war flail Strike; **Effect** The ash giant wraps the chain of the flail around the target and grinds its flesh. The creature takes 2d10 slashing damage and 2d8 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) with a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. **Tangle-Topple** The ash giant makes a piggy clod Strike. If it hits, the target is tangled in ragged scrap. It's [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), can't leave the ground, and falls to the ground if it's flying. This ends if the creature [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) or the metal is [Forced Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) (DC 28).
Chitinous Chariots Ash giants ride giant insects and other vermin to battle. They especially love to cover their mounts' exoskeletons with metal harnesses, armor plates, and jagged spikes added just for sadism's sake. Mounts they use frequently include the [ankhrav hive mother](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2823) and [deadly mantis](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3095). Smaller creatures, including the narrik and the shriezyx, are harnessed into teams to pull forward their roving mechanisms of war.
name: Ash Giant

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Tomb Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4411)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Masters of mausoleums and keepers of crypts, the dread creatures called tomb giants are anathema to all living beings, but especially so to other types of giants. Long ago, so the legends say, tomb giants sold their souls in exchange for unfathomable power over the undead. Tomb giants construct massive gothic settlements in haunted valleys and on forsaken hillsides, far enough away from the societies of smaller people that they remain relatively undisturbed, but close enough that they can raid the graveyards of nearby villages with impunity. Elder tomb giants enjoy the thrill of subduing, slaying, and reanimating their fellow giants. A tomb giant views its mortal life as only one part of its existence. After death, most tomb giants are reanimated as undead, who then continue to practice their necromantic arts.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 30

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Giant_TombGIant.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 12">[Tomb Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4411)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Giant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=616" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 164

**Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) (imprecise) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +25, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +21

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +3

**Con** +6

**Int** +3

**Wis** +7

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Items**
_[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=394)_, black onyx gems worth 300 gp

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 32 

**Fort** +22 

**Ref** +19 

**Will** +25 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 255
([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
scythe +27 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 2d10+13 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 3d6+13 slashing plus dooming touch

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rock +24 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 3d8+13 bludgeoning

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 32
- **5th**
[Bind Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1449) (×3), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×3)

**Rituals** DC 32

- **2nd**
[Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=117)

**Dooming Touch**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The tomb giant's claws carry the accursed power of their foul gods. A creature hit by the tomb giant's claw Strike becomes [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1.

**Font of Death** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The tomb giant turns the spiritual tide on a creature that has just died, temporarily transforming it into a volatile vessel powered by [the Void](/Planes.aspx?ID=28). The tomb giant touches a creature that died within the past 24 hours, infusing its flesh and bone with void energy. Once during the next hour, the tomb giant can spend a single action (from any distance) to release this void from the corpse in an explosion that deals 10d8 void damage in a 15-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save); if not released before the end of the hour, the energy dissipates harmlessly. The tomb giant can't use Font of Death while a previous corpse remains infused.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-420" />
name: Tomb Giant
search_markdown:
<traits>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Tide Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4412)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

On secluded beaches where the waves lap across pristine, untouched sands, tide giants dwell in solitude at the shore. They aim to live in quiet serenity, appreciating the beauty that surrounds them. Tide giants thrive on lounging on the sand, living slow while taking in the sights, sounds, and smells of the shore, all while enjoying bowls of juice and rum. Many live by the philosophy that their precious and sacred connection to the magic of the sea is deepened by enjoying the water, such as by watching the sun reflect off the waves cresting the shore. Interruptions, trespassers, and other surprises upset tide giants' calm massively, turning their placid attitude into an enraged hurricane.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 31

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Tide_Giant.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 13">[Tide Giant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4412)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=529" />
<trait label="Giant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=616" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=732" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 165

**Perception** +21; low-light vision

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +21, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +23

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +6

**Con** +6

**Int** +0

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Items**
_+[1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2845) [trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=401)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 33 

**Fort** +23 

**Ref** +25 

**Will** +21 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 250

</row>

**Resistances**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10

**Cloak of High Tide** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) 10 feet. Elemental water magic ebbs and flows into a tide giant. At the start of each of the giant's turns in combat, their cloak of high tide automatically activates if it's inactive or ends if it's already active. Any creature other than a tide giant that enters or starts its turn in the aura while it's active regains 5 HP; this is a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) effect, and a creature can benefit from it only once per round. When active, the cloak appears as a magical, flowing cloak of seafoam that billows from the tidal giant's shoulders and the back of their limbs. The cloak is inactive when the tide giant isn't in combat.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, swim 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
trident +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d8+14 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 2d4+14 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
trident +26 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 2d8+14 piercing

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 33
- **5th**
[Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473)

**Blood Tide** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The tide giant [Swims](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), or Swims twice if their cloak of high tide is active. Holding out their trident, they slash those they pass, dealing 2d8 piercing damage and 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) to each enemy the giant moves within 10 feet of during their movement. Each creature can be affected only once during a single use of Blood Tide.

**Tine and Tide** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) A wave blasts from the giant's trident in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) or a 5-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 100 feet. If the giant's cloak of high tide is active, this is a 30-foot cone or a 10-foot burst. Each creature in the area takes 9d8 bludgeoning damage with a DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">On The Beach</title>

Despite being able to dwell entirely underwater, tide giants favor building their homes near beaches to advantageously use land and sea as they wish. Their bungalows are open to the elements. They’re also quite cluttered, with treasures that washed ashore heaped in disorganized piles of driftwood, fish skeletons, seashells, broken bottles, and more.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-420" />
name: Tide Giant

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Girtablilu Seer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4414)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The highest position of authority in a girtablilu community is the group's religious leader. This sacred duty requires years of study, meditation, and dedication to awaken a mystical font of divine magic within the girtablilu. These seers ensure that every action taken by the community falls in line with even the most obscure dogma of their tradition, and they don't hesitate to turn their fury on members of their own community who violate their mandates.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**

<column gap="tiny">

<traits>
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=523" />
<trait label="Goblin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=618" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 168

**Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=160); snake empathy

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +6, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +7, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +4

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +4

**Con** +2

**Int** -2

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Snake Empathy** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A goblin snake can communicate with snakes.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +4 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 15

</row>

**Coiled Strike** <actions string="Reaction" /> As [Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48), but the goblin snake can use this reaction only if it's Coiled.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 5 feet, swim 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +9 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d6+2 piercing

**Coil** <actions string="Single Action" />  The goblin snake uses an action to coil itself, increasing its reach with its fangs from 5 to 10 feet. After the goblin snake Strikes with its fangs, it becomes uncoiled.

**Goblin Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The goblin snake belches a cloud of nauseating vapor in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Non-[goblin](/Traits.aspx?ID=618) creatures within the cloud must succeed at a DC 16 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. On a critical failure, a creature is also [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for as long as it is sickened. Creatures that successfully save are immune for 24 hours. The goblin snake can't use Goblin Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>
