These bizarre mutant goblin snakes possess no fewer than three heads. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Gobmob Snake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4416) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 168 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=160); snake empathy **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +8 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** -2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 **Snake Empathy** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A gobmob snake can communicate with snakes. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +13 **Will** +8 **HP** 60 **Incessant Yammering** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642)) 15 feet. A gobmob snake's heads constantly bicker and snipe at one another, annoying and distracting anyone nearby. Each non-goblin creature that begins its turn in the aura must attempt a DC 21 Will save. On a failure, it takes a –1 status penalty to Perception checks and Will saves for 1 round. On a success, it is temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Infighting** Whenever a gobmob snake critically fails at an attack roll or skill check, it must succeed at a DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) or become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 as its heads argue over which of them is to blame. An enemy can provoke an argument by attempting a DC 20 [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check as a single action with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits.
**Coiled Strike** As [Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48), but the gobmob snake can use this reaction only if it's Coiled. --- **Speed** 25 feet, burrow 5 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** fangs +14 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 2d6+5 piercing **Coil** The gobmob snake uses an action to coil itself, increasing its reach with its fangs from 5 to 10 feet. After the goblin snake Strikes with its fangs, it becomes uncoiled. **Goblin Breath** ([Olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The gobmob snake belches a cloud of nauseating vapor in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Non-goblin creatures within the cloud must succeed at a DC 20 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. On a critical failure, a creature is also [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for as long as it is sickened. Creatures that successfully save are immune for 24 hours. The goblin snake can't use Goblin Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Gobmob Snake

markdown:
Long ago, the priests of ancient religions mastered a technique to bring them closer to their god: the animation of clay into a living being. These clay beings, called golems, are animated by placing a piece of paper with the name of the priest's god within the golem's mouth (or, in some cases, by carving a holy word on the golem's forehead). Though they are intelligent as a result of their divine blessing, golems generally don't act unless commanded to, and they rarely have the capacity for speech, a gift the gods bestowed on their creations alone. Instead, they use their intelligence to facilitate their maker's communion with their god in divine rites unknown outside of a religion's most faithful. In some oppressed communities, golems serve as protectors against outside violence, fighting as a last resort but aiming to defuse situations first. Other times, the golem operates as a guardian to a temple or helper to a holy person. In rare cases, golems have been known to go on rampages when they aren't allowed a day of rest every week. These rampages can be extremely destructive to the communities the golems otherwise protect, so makers must endeavor to take good care of their creations. Golem legends are diverse and widespread across the Inner Sea region and beyond. Some tell of even stranger powers bestowed on the golem, such as the ability to summon the spirits of the dead. Golems with such abilities are undoubtedly created by the most holy of priests, attuned to their god beyond what most mortals could hope to accomplish. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Golem](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4417) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 169 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +20, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +12 **Will** +16 **HP** 170 **Immunities** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal attacks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2311), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Day of Rest** A golem needs 1 day of rest per week or it becomes uncontrollable. An uncontrollable golem is unable to cast spells, and it takes a –2 circumstance penalty to checks made using Wisdom, including Will saves. While uncontrollable, the golem loses its immunity to [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696). The golem is uncontrollable until it takes a day of rest.
**Faithful** A golem faithfully serves its creator as long as it's not in an uncontrollable state (see above). While the golem is faithful, it follows the commands of its creator, even to its own detriment. While the golem remains faithful to its creator, the golem can't be confused or controlled by any creature other than its creator.
**Hefty Helper** The golem can carry 13 Bulk before becoming [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70) and can carry a maximum Bulk of 18. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** fist +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+8 bludgeoning plus consecrated fists **Divine Innate Spells** DC 24, attack +16 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689) - **2nd** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×4) - **3rd** [Calm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458) (×2), [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557) (at will) - **4th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499) (×2) **Consecrated Fists** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) After the golem casts a [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) spell, their Strikes deal an additional 1d8 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage and gain the [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) and holy traits. These benefits last until the end of the golem's next turn. **Rampage** **Requirements** The golem is uncontrollable; **Effect** The golem makes a melee Strike against every creature in its reach, whether that creature is an ally or not. The attacks count toward its multiple attack penalty normally, but the penalty does not increase until after all the Strikes are complete.
Temple Guardians Golems have been used to guard and assist at temples across the Inner Sea region, though they're said to have originated in northern Garund. Only followers of holy deities can create golems, most of which are given life by priests of [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281) or [Shelyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=293), or sometimes [Casandalee](/Deities.aspx?ID=335).
name: Golem

Graveknight captains once proudly led squads of troops in battle. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Graveknight Captain](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4418) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 172 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +12, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Items** _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)_, _graveknight's shield_, [Warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=403) --- **AC** 24 (26 with shield raised) **Fort** +16 **Ref** +14 **Will** +13 **HP** 90 (rejuvenation, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) [**Graveknight's Shield**](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=423)
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** warhammer +16 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 2d8+7 bludgeoning plus 1d6 acid **Melee** fist +16 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d4+7 bludgeoning plus 1d6 acid **Devastating Blast** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The graveknight captain unleashes a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of energy. Creatures in the area take 4d12 acid with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The graveknight captain can use this ability once every 1d4 rounds. **Graveknight's Curse** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566)) This curse affects anyone who wears a graveknight's armor for at least 1 hour; **Saving Throw** DC 24 Will save; Onset 1 hour; **Stage 1** [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1 and can't remove armor (1 day); **Stage 2** doomed 2, –10- foot status penalty to Speeds, and can't remove armor (1 day); **Stage 3** dies and transforms into the armor's graveknight. **Ruinous Weapons** Any weapon or unarmed attack the graveknight uses gains the effects of a _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon_ and a [_caustic_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2834) weapon rune. **Weapon Master** The graveknight captain has access to the [critical specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2203) effects of any weapons they wield.
name: Graveknight Captain

[Exemplars](/Classes.aspx?ID=65) of undying violence, graveknight warmasters are devastating forces on the battlefield, able to spur allies to ever greater levels of violence. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Graveknight Warmaster](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4419) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 172 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +28, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +28, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +24, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +25, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** _[+1 resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)_, [Heavy Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=428) (20 bolts), [War Flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=402) --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +24 **Will** +24 **HP** 255 (rejuvenation, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Hungry Armor** A creature that Strikes a graveknight warmaster with a melee weapon must succeed at a DC 31 Reflex save or be disarmed of that weapon. If the creature critically fails, the weapon ends up in the graveknight's space. A creature that hits a graveknight warmaster with an unarmed attack must succeed at a DC 31 Reflex save or become [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) by the graveknight until the end of its next turn, when it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296), or when the graveknight moves, whichever comes first.
**Sacrilegious Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) 30 feet. When a creature in the aura uses a [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) spell or ability, the graveknight warmaster automatically attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) it, with a +23 counteract modifier.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _war flail_ +29 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 3d10+14 bludgeoning plus 1d6 electricity **Melee** fist +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d6+14 bludgeoning plus 1d6 electricity **Ranged** heavy crossbow +25 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 2](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 3d10+6 piercing plus 1d6 electricity **Devastating Blast** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)) The graveknight warmaster unleashes a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of energy. Creatures in the area take 8d12 electricity with a DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The graveknight warmaster can use this ability once every 1d4 rounds. **Exemplar of Violence** ([Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The graveknight attempts a Strike as their armor flashes with sinister power that spurs allies to violence. After the Strike, allies who can see the graveknight can use a reaction to [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), but they must end this movement in a space adjacent to an enemy. One ally of the graveknight's choice can instead use a reaction to Strike. **Graveknight's Curse** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566)) This curse affects anyone who wears a graveknight's armor for at least 1 hour; **Saving Throw** DC 39 Will save; Onset 1 hour; **Stage 1** [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1 and can't remove armor (1 day); **Stage 2** doomed 2, –10- foot status penalty to Speeds, and can't remove armor (1 day); **Stage 3** dies and transforms into the armor's graveknight. **Phantom Mount** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The graveknight warmaster summons a supernatural mount, as [_marvelous mount_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1594) heightened to a 7th rank. Unlike _marvelous mount_, the steed's AC and saving throw bonuses are all 4 lower than the graveknight's, and the steed has AC 34, Fort +23, Ref +20, Will +20, and 85 Hit Points. If the steed is destroyed, the graveknight warmaster must wait 1 hour before using this ability again.; the steed has **Ruinous Weapons** Any weapon or unarmed attack the graveknight uses gains the effects of a _[+1 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon_ and a _[greater shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847)_ weapon rune. **Weapon Master** The graveknight captain has access to the [critical specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2203) effects of any weapons they wield.
name: Graveknight Warmaster

Once the servant of a deity, the graveknight champion returned as an undead after a life cut short in service to their god. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Graveknight Champion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4420) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 173 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +31, [Deity Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +29, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=435) (20 arrows), _[+2 resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)_, [Greatpick](/Weapons.aspx?ID=378) --- **AC** 38 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +26 **Will** +25 **HP** 275 (rejuvenation, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Clutching Armor** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Trigger** A creature attempts to move away from the graveknight champion; **Effect** The graveknight champion's armor animates and attempts to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the triggering creature. It makes an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) at +29. The armor can continue to Grapple the creature normally. Since the armor is grappling the creature, the graveknight doesn't need a free hand to do so.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _greatpick_ +30 ([fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=597), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d10+16 slashing plus 1d6 fire **Melee** fist +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d6+16 bludgeoning plus 1d6 fire **Ranged** _composite shortbow_ +28 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 3d6+10 piercing plus 1d6 fire **Innate Divine Spells** DC 33, attack +25 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **4th** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) - **5th** [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1464), [Divine Immolation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1496) - **6th** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Spiritual Armament](/Spells.aspx?ID=1687) - **7th** [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=1508), [Execute](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519) **Channel Magic** The graveknight champion redirects magical energies through its armor, allowing it to deliver magic through an attack. The graveknight champion Casts a Spell that takes 1 or 2 actions to cast and requires a spell attack modifier. The effects of the spell don't occur immediately but are imbued into an attack instead. The graveknight champion then makes a melee Strike with a weapon or unarmed attack. The spell is coupled with the attack, using the attack roll result to determine the effects of both the Strike and the spell. This counts as two attacks for the graveknight's multiple attack penalty but doesn't apply the penalty until after they've completed Channeling Magic. The graveknight champion can't use Channel Magic again for 1d4 rounds. **Devastating Blast** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The graveknight champion unleashes a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of energy. Creatures in the area take 9d12 fire with a DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The graveknight champion can use this ability once every 1d4 rounds. **Graveknight's Curse** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566)) This curse affects anyone who wears a graveknight champion's armor for at least 1 hour; **Saving Throw** DC 40 Will save; **Onset** 1 hour; **Stage 1** [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1 and can't remove armor (1 day); **Stage 2** doomed 2, –10- foot status penalty to Speeds, and can't remove armor (1 day); **Stage 3** dies and transforms into the armor's graveknight. **Ruinous Weapons** Any weapon or unarmed attack the graveknight uses gains the effects of a _[+1 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon_ and a _[greater flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838)_ weapon rune. **Weapon Master** The graveknight has access to the [critical specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2203) effects of any weapons they wield.
name: Graveknight Champion

markdown:
Green men are ancient, enigmatic lesser deities of the primeval forests, living embodiments of nature and plantkind. When a forest or other woodland terrain generates enough nature spirits, beings with the same sort of vital essence that embodies leshies or answers the call of a commune, they naturally coalesce together and apotheosize into a green man. Green men aren't concerned with all the multifarious processes of nature like [Gozreh](/Deities.aspx?ID=284) or many other nature deities. Instead, they focus nearly all their attention on the plants of their home, only concerning themselves with animals, minerals, and the like insomuch as they affect the plants. Despite their name, green men aren't necessarily male; as creatures of pure natural power, to many of them, the concept of gender holds no meaning, and to those that do, they can be of any gender. Most green men tend to ignore animals, which to them include sapient creatures such as humans. However, green men who involve themselves in the affairs of such creatures do exist. These individuals are far more likely to attempt to spread their influence far and wide, either for good or ill. Virtuous green men provide succor to all that come within their home, not only to plants, providing wisdom like a nurturing parent. Wicked green men, however, allow rare and dangerous plants to thrive in their domains by spreading fear and devastation to all those who might threaten plant life, though they might keep a few animals around to hunt for sport. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 53 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 51 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 48 [Green Man](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4421) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 174 **Perception** +42; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), plantsense 60 feet **Languages** [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Wildsong](/Languages.aspx?ID=116); green tongue **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +39, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +40, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +40, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +40, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +40, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +48, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +41, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +44 **Str** +12 **Dex** +9 **Con** +11 **Int** +7 **Wis** +10 **Cha** +8 **Green Tongue** A green man can communicate with plants, with the effects of [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681), and can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on plants and [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) things from plants.
**Plantsense** A green man can sense life force via plants. This allows them to observe a living or undead creature's vital essence within 60 feet of the green man, but they can also use this precise sense to observe any living or undead creature within 60 feet of any plant matter within 120 feet of the green man. This allows the green man to see living things through solid plant matter, as well as seeing through other barriers if there are plants on the other side. --- **AC** 51 **Fort** +43 **Ref** +39 **Will** +42 **HP** 525 **Resistances** bludgeoning 20, piercing 20 **Weaknesses** [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 20, fire 20 **Green Caress** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) 60 feet. Living creatures in the area other than plants slowly transform into non-creature plants. The green man can exclude creatures from this effect, but they must be aware of a creature's presence and location to do so. A non-plant creature in the area must attempt a DC 45 Fortitude save immediately before the start of its turn.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected, or if it is slowed by green caress, it reduces its slowed value by 2.
**Success** The creature is unaffected, or if it is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) by green caress, it reduces its slowed value by 1.
**Failure** The creature becomes slowed 1, or if it was already slowed by green caress, increases the slowed value by 1, as their body transforms more and more into a non-creature plant. If the creature ever becomes slowed to the point they have no actions left for their turn, they become an inanimate plant, a condition that can only be reversed by [_manifestation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592) or similarly powerful magic.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature becomes slowed 2 (or increases the condition value by 2).
**Root In Place** **Trigger** A creature within the green man's reach uses a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action or leaves a square during a move action it's using; **Effect** The green man lashes out to hold the foe in place. The green man makes a vine Strike against the triggering creature. If the attack hits, the green man disrupts the action. If the creature was [Flying](/Rules.aspx?ID=3374) when its action was disrupted, it falls. --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 40 feet **Melee** vine +46 ([deadly 3d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 4d10+27 bludgeoning plus Absorb Magic and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Ranged** thorn +43 ([fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=597), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 4d8+27 piercing plus embed **Primal Innate Spells** DC 48 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **8th** [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) - **9th** [Energy Aegis](/Spells.aspx?ID=1512) - **10th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×3), [Manifestation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592) (×3), [Nature's Pathway](/Spells.aspx?ID=1613) (at will), [Regenerate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1648) (×3), [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Rituals** DC 48 - **4th** [Plant Growth](/Rituals.aspx?ID=121) - **6th** [Awaken Animal](/Rituals.aspx?ID=109), [Commune](/Rituals.aspx?ID=114), [Primal Call](/Rituals.aspx?ID=122) - **8th** [Control Weather](/Rituals.aspx?ID=116) **Absorb Magic** The green man's vines leach away magic and transform it into life essence for the green man. On a successful vine Strike, the green man attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) one spell active on the target (typically one vexing the green man, or determined randomly if they aren't aware of specific effects), with a counteract rank of 10 and a modifier of +38. If the effect is counteracted, the green man gains 30 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 10 minutes. **Embed** The green man's thorns embed themselves into any creature they damage, taking root into the ground. A target damaged by a thorn has its Speeds halved, and it can't [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or otherwise leave the ground until the thorn is removed. Removing a thorn requires 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions, which don't have to be consecutive. If the creature performing the final action doesn't succeed at a DC 45 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) check as part of that action, the target takes 10d6 piercing damage upon the thorn's removal. **Focus Vines** The green man focuses all their vines against a single vexing foe, making a single vine Strike. On a success, the target takes 5d10 additional bludgeoning damage and is affected by Absorb Magic three times. Even on a failure, the target takes the normal effects of a hit with a vine Strike, but on a critical failure, the vines miss completely. **Green Grab** A green man can use their [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) action against a creature of any size. **Green Rituals** A green man can perform all their rituals without secondary casters, relying on their own primal ties to the vital essence in spirits of nature. A green man's [awaken animal](/Rituals.aspx?ID=109) and [primal call](/Rituals.aspx?ID=122) rituals work on plants instead of their usual range of choices. Most green men also know the ritual to create various types of [leshies](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=437) and possibly even magic allowing the creation of [arboreals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=364) or more powerful [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) creatures. **Vine Forest** The green man lashes out with all six vines to attack many opponents. They make up to six vine Strikes, each against a different target; this counts as one attack for their multiple attack penalty, increasing only after all the attacks are made.
Leshies And Green Men Green men are sometimes called “[leshy](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=437) kings” in ancient manuscripts. [Druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=34) consider the first green men to be the original source of the rituals for creating the less-deific leshies, and possibly even [arboreals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=364) and other intelligent plants. The affinity between leshies and green men is mutual. Leshies are the most likely ancestry to worship green men, and green men are particularly fond of their less-powerful kin.
Worshipping Green Men Individual [green men](/Deities.aspx?ID=666) are lesser deities, capable of granting spells to those who worship them. Green men typically only allow intelligent plants—such as [leshies](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=437)—to be their [clerics](/Classes.aspx?ID=33). If another creature proves to be a friend of plants, after a thorough personal vetting, a green man might wholeheartedly accept this strange fleshy worshipper into the fold. While individual green men have different edicts and anathema befitting their personalities, the following is a baseline most worshippers of green men follow.
name: Green Man

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Grimple](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4422)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Even more than most gremlins, grimples resent the trappings of civilization: inn common rooms with their rowdy singalongs, livery yards with their whinnying horses, church steeples with their clanging bells. Grimples live to spoil these conventions, dropping tavern signs on patrons, urinating in rain barrels, and opening stable doors. When all else fails, they literally vomit their disdain on passersby. 

Grimples resemble humanoid, mange-ridden opossums, with boar-like tusks that aid them in rooting through garbage heaps for food. They're agile climbers who glide from eave to eave on the loose flaps of skin between their limbs. Savvy gremlin-hunters know to look for the skin flakes and fur grimples shed from their parasite-infested hides.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Gremlin_Grimple.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature -1">[Grimple](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4422)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=599" />
<trait label="Gremlin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=756" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 176

**Perception** +6; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118)

**Skills**
[Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +5, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +2, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +5, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +3

**Int** +1

**Wis** +2

**Cha** -2
</row>

**Items**
satchel with 5 rocks

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 14 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +4 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 9

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 2

**Gremlin Lice** Whenever a living creature touches or is touched by a grimple (including via a successful unarmed melee Strike), it must succeed at a DC 13 Reflex save or become infested by gremlin lice. While infested, the targeted creature is distracted by the itching sensation and is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1, though it can use an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=3259) action to scratch at the itching lice to suppress the stupefied condition from the lice for 1d4 rounds. The infestation ends after 24 hours or until the creature is submerged in water or exposed to a [severe cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) environment, whichever comes first.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, climb 20 feet, fly 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bite +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rock +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 bludgeoning

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 16
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715)
- **1st**
[Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=1547)

**Putrid Vomit** <actions string="Single Action" />  The grimple spews a 30-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) of vomit. Each creature in the line must succeed at a DC 16 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The grimple can't use Putrid Vomit again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Grimple Allies</title>

Grimples sometimes team up with other [gremlins](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=424) to cause trouble. Stronger gremlins, such as [jinkins](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3033), use cranky grimples to lure victims into dangerous traps. Left to their own devices, grimples bully [mitflits](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3031) or train [giant rats](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3162) (Monster Core 288) and [spider swarms](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3206) to do their bidding.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-424" />
name: Grimple

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Haniver](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4423)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hanivers are the most benign type of gremlin—capricious fey who skim the waves on rubbery fin-wings. Though they don't actively sabotage their environment, sailors bemoan these gremlins' grasping fingers. Hanivers' incessant curiosity compels them to examine any object that draws their attention. Such treasures might include overturned fruit baskets, sacks of coins, or the gleaming teeth of a shark (often still in the shark's mouth, to the fey's regret). Should hanivers like what they find, they steal it, making them a bane to fishers and dockworkers everywhere.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=529" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=599" />
<trait label="Gremlin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=756" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 176

**Perception** +5; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +5, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +4, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +3, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +5, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** -1

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +4 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +3 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 8

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 2

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 20 feet, swim 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bite +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 piercing

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 13
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639)
- **1st**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) (at will)

**Rearrange Possessions** <actions string="Single Action or Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The haniver attempts to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410) a small object off a target's person. If they succeed, they also rifle through and rearrange the contents of the target's pockets, pouches, and other containers. The next time the target attempts to draw a weapon or retrieve a worn item, doing so requires 2 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions instead of one. The haniver can Steal an object that's closely guarded using this action without the –5 penalty, though not objects that would be extremely noticeable or time-consuming to remove. They can spend 2 actions instead of 1 to use this ability to Steal from a creature in combat or otherwise on guard.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Mari Haniver</title>

Sailors sometimes affix a “Mari Haniver” to their ship's prow. Made from a ray's dried carcass, this clever bit of taxidermy resembles a dead haniver enough to scare gremlins away. But should a haniver wise up to the ruse, it will stop at nothing to torment its would-be deceivers.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-424" />
name: Haniver

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Fuath](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4424)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Despite their small size, fuaths are shipwreckers incarnate. In the dark of night, these gremlins sever ratlines, slash sails, smash sextants, and soil provisions. Once the ship founders, the fuaths return to indulge their craving for land-raised flesh. While fuaths prefer to take their prey asleep, they save a terrible doom for sailors who attack them, surrounding the mariners' faces in magically congealed water to drown them where they stand. 

Constantly dripping with water, fuaths have seahorse-like faces, seaweedgreen fur over yellow skin, and wicked lobster claws for hands. Lacking the Sakvroth tongue, they have trouble relating to other gremlins aside from hanivers, but they revere sea hags and wicked aquatic fey.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 2, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 2

**Vulnerable to Sunlight** A fuath becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (or increases its drained condition by 1) after every consecutive hour they're exposed to sunlight. Being submerged in more than a foot of water prevents the sunlight from harming the fuath.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dart +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 piercing

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 17
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639)
- **1st**
[Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675)

**Viscous Choke** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The fuath surrounds the head of one air-breathing creature within 30 feet in a magical film of viscous water for 1 minute. The target must succeed at a DC 17 Reflex save or it begins to choke and must hold its breath to avoid [drowning](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439). The film can be temporarily wiped away with a total of 3 Interact actions by the choking creature or creatures adjacent to it, allowing a new Reflex save with a +2 circumstance bonus to end the effect. (These actions don't need to be consecutive or made by the same creature.)

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Fuath Guardians</title>

Lone fuaths sometimes appoint themselves guardians of nature, protecting spawning grounds from overfishing or preventing careless cutting of peat bogs. Most, however, are unrepentant saboteurs.
</aside>

The first grioths encountered on a new world are scouts. After traveling from their home worlds across the vastness of space via one-way portals, grioth scouts never expect to see their homes again, as grioth leadership ensures true devotion to the colonization effort by stranding scouts on their new planets. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 17 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 [Grioth Scout](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4425) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 178 **Perception** +7; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), echolocation (precise) 20 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Grioth](/Languages.aspx?ID=65); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 30 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +7 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 Echolocation A grioth can use their hearing as a precise sense at the listed range. **Items** [voidglass kukri](/Weapons.aspx?ID=413) --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +5 **Ref** +9 **Will** +7 **HP** 18 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 3 **[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=68)**
**No Breath** A grioth doesn't breathe except to speak and is immune to effects that require breathing (such as an inhaled poison). --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** kukri +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 1d6 slashing **Melee** jaws +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d4 piercing plus grioth venom **Occult Innate Spells** DC 16, attack +8 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **1st** [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632) **Grioth Venom** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 17 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** frightened 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** frightened 3 (1 round) **Shock Mind** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The grioth scout makes a Strike with a voidglass weapon. If the Strike hits, it deals an additional 1d6 mental damage, and the target must succeed at a DC 17 Will save (this has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait) or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.

markdown:
Grioth cultists represent the lowest tier of their remorseless cult— worshippers of the Outer Gods and Great Old Ones tasked with leading scouting missions into new worlds. While they're ranked above scouts in the chain of command, they occupy a lowly place in the overall religious hierarchy. Though whispered rumors describe the legendary rituals needed to wrench worlds from their suns, these cultists don't learn this potent magic. Such secrets are left to grioth high priests, who are always the last to arrive on a colonized world. Most grioths worship the Outer God [Nyarlathotep](/Deities.aspx?ID=684) in one of his many incarnations, although some worship other entities such as [Azathoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=567), [Nhimbaloth](/Deities.aspx?ID=572), or [Yog-Sothoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=580). In the rare cases where a grioth is cut off from their society and given the opportunity to explore other religions, they still tend to choose faiths associated with the stars or the night, such as [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281) or [Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=296). These grioths are loathed by others of their own kind, who view them as dangerous heretics. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Grioth Cultist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4426) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 179 **Perception** +10; [greater darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410), echolocation (precise) 20 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Grioth](/Languages.aspx?ID=65); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 30 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +8, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +9, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10 **Str** +0 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Echolocation** A grioth can use their hearing as a precise sense at the listed range. **Items** [voidglass kukri](/Weapons.aspx?ID=413) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +10 **Will** +10 **HP** 40 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5 **[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=68)**
**No Breath** A grioth doesn't breathe except to speak and is immune to effects that require breathing (such as an inhaled poison). --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** kukri +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 1d6+2 slashing **Melee** jaws +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing plus grioth venom **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 20, attack +12 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Forbidding Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) - **2nd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Noise Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 19, attack +11 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **2nd** [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632) **Grioth Venom** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** frightened 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** frightened 3 (1 round) **Invoke Haunter of the Dark** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The grioth cultist waves a hand in a complex pattern to invoke dark powers, dealing 3d8 mental damage. Each nongrioth creature within 20 feet must attempt a DC 20 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and becomes stupefied 1 for 1 minute. **Shock Mind** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The grioth scout makes a Strike with a voidglass weapon. If the Strike hits, it deals an additional 2d6 mental damage, and the target must succeed at a DC 20 Will save (this has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait) or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.

markdown:
The appearance of the mighty grogrisant is a once-in-a-generation event, but the descendants of those legendary beasts are well known along the World's Edge Mountains and throughout Taldor. These primal predators don't travel in a pride as mundane lions do. Instead, they avoid others of their kind, even to mate, and seek out ordinary lions once a year for this purpose. A grisantian lioness who bears cubs only tends her offspring long enough for them to become self-sufficient—which takes but a few months, thanks to their kind's incredibly rapid growth and development. A full-grown grisantian lion is as large as an elephant and exceedingly aggressive, hunting anything it comes across. While ordinary lions rely on stealth and pack tactics to secure a meal, the grisantian lion is too big to hide amid tall grass. Instead, it has adapted to the mountains, where it chooses a large, hard-to-reach cave as its home, often killing any creature unfortunate enough to already inhabit the place. A grisantian lion can track for miles and is a canny hunter, hiding along rocky cliffs and outcroppings as it stalks prey. Although they're wild creatures that can never be tamed, grisantian lions understand Taldane and occasionally agree to help those who defend nature. However, such alliances are temporary and unreliable at best. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Grisantian Lion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4427) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 180 **Perception** +25; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +22, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +22 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** -3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +22 **Will** +19 **HP** 215 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10, physical 10 (except bludgeoning) --- **Speed** 35 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** jaws +26, **Damage** 3d10+14 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 3d8+12 slashing **Blinding Mane** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day **Effect** Drawing upon the power of their grogrisant ancestor, the grisantian lion focuses and causes their mane to glow with bright light. All creatures within 20 feet must attempt a DC 29 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until its next turn begins.
**Failure** The target is blinded for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The target is blinded permanently. **Dual Pounce** The grisantian lion Strides and makes two claw Strikes against the same creature at the end of that movement. Each attack counts against the grisantian lion's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after the grisantian lion makes both attacks. If both attacks hit, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73)** claw. If the grisantian lion Rends after a successful Dual Pounce, combine the Rend's damage with that from the Dual Pounce for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses. **Vicious Rend** **Trigger** The grisantian lion uses [Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73); **Effect** The target's armor takes damage equal to the damage from Rend. The target can attempt a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save to reduce this damage.
The Legend Of The Grogrisant Early tales of the founding of Taldor speak of the [Grogrisant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1727), an enormous six-eyed lion with a mane that glowed the like the sun. The lion destroyed the city-states of the region, feeding on their livestock and plundering their wealth to line its den. The great hero Taldaris finally slew the terrible beast and went on to become the First Emperor of Taldor. Terrifying six-eyed lions reminiscent of the original make repeated appearances throughout Taldor’s history only to meet their demise at the hands of heroes. While the original Grogrisant of legend is honored with a capitalized name, the remaining grogrisants of history bear the less formal name for the sake of classification.
name: Grisantian Lion

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Grodair](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4428)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Grodairs are bizarre fishlike creatures native to the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35). Their supernatural physiology includes a strange organ with an extradimensional space capable of storing thousands of gallons of water. This grants grodairs the ability to create a suitable environment for themselves almost anywhere by releasing the water and quickly transforming the surrounding area into a shallow bog. When grodairs wish to leave for a new location, they simply suck all the water back up. When necessary, grodairs can walk on land by using the long, fleshy tentacles that dangle from their bellies.

Grodairs have two sets of eyes that function independently. While this makes the creatures difficult to surprise, it also makes them easy to distract. During conversations, they have a tendency to lose focus quickly and have trouble remembering things. Still, they're avid conversationalists and enjoy hearing tales about the world. While this makes grodairs quite likable, they aren't reliable. They have difficulty sticking to plans or schedules, and they can keep a secret only if they happen to forget it first. For these reasons, grodairs don't make the best allies.

Grodairs can capture and store within their throats any small animals and plants easily suspended in water, and then eject them for consumption at a later time. They're voracious and curious omnivores who love trying new food—in fact, the pursuit of new and interesting meals is the most common reason they venture from the First World. This, fortunately, makes grodairs easy to negotiate with, something of a necessity when people need to relocate one of the creatures (along with their bog) away from roads and fields meant for farming.

Due to their ability to transport so much water, some desert settlements have attempted to pay grodairs to form oases along trade routes. Unfortunately, the grodairs' short attention span leads them to forget why they were in the desert in the first place and wander off.

([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20

</column>

</row>

**Languages**

</row>

**Death Flood** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) When a grodair dies, their body explodes in a blast of pressurized water that deals 4d6 bludgeoning damage to creatures within a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that fails its save is pushed 5 feet away from the grodair's corpse (10 feet on a critical failure).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, swim 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +15,
**Damage** 2d8+7 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +15,
**Damage** 1d10+7 bludgeoning plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46)

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
water jet +13 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 3d6 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) 10 feet

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 22
- **5th**
[Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473)

**Muddy Field** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The grodair transforms all soil, sand, or similar sediment in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) into mud for 1 round. This mud is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for creatures other than grodairs.

**Organ of Endless Water** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The grodair causes water to pour through its scales from a magical sac in its body, either at a rate of 1 gallon per round or in a 5-foot-long stream at a rate of 5 gallons per round. It can stop the flow of water as a single action.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Grodair Treasure</title>

Amid the vile, rubbery entrails of an exploded grodair is a cluster of tubular organs the size of a melon which contains their extradimensional water storage. Harvesting the organ cluster takes 5 minutes and a successful DC 22 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) check. For the next 2d6 hours, the cluster can then be used to produce water in the same manner as a grodair's Organ of Endless Water ability. However, on a critical failure to harvest the organ cluster, it bursts and deals 2d6 bludgeoning damage to the harvester.
</aside>
name: Grodair

markdown:
Stone lion cubs are curious, playful, and occasionally mischief makers. Despite their still developing abilities, they eagerly hone their skills with make-believe missions. Many hope that they'll one day become responsible guardians like their parents, though if these stone beasts age at all, they do so on a scale imperceivable to most mortals. Smaller in stature and ferocity, stone lion cubs sometimes try to guard places of smaller importance, including facsimiles of the same kinds of structures their parents attend. They're less than dependable as guardians, as they have the attention span and rambunctious nature of children. Their parents often need to call them to heel, ordering them to stand at the parent's side as parent and child both stay motionless for the rising day. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Stone Lion Cub](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4429) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 182 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 60 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +7, [Meteorology Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +7, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +8 **Str** +3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 **Anchored Soul** The stone lion cub is mystically bonded to their bonded vessel and must remain within 1 mile of it. Some might be further restricted to the location they guard. --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +7 **Will** +10 +1 status to all saves vs. unholy **HP** 28 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) Bonded Vessel The condition of a stone cub's vessel dictates the cub's maximum Hit Point value. Undamaged, the vessel is an object with 28 Hit Points ([BT](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) 14). When the cub is in spirit form, damaging it doesn't hurt the vessel, but damaging the vessel deals an equal amount of damage to the cub. When the cub Inhabits its Vessel they're a single target, and damage reduces the Hit Points of both the cub and the vessel. If the vessel is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), the cub can still fight normally while inhabiting it and takes no ill effect, but if the vessel is ever destroyed, the cub is instantly slain and can't reconstitute.
**Reconstitution** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When the cub reaches 0 Hit Points, its spirit dissipates. If its bonded vessel is intact, the cub re-forms in this vessel after 2d4 days, fully healed. If the vessel is broken, it must first be Repaired, after which the cub reforms in 3d4 days. --- **Speed** fly 25 feet **Inhabit Vessel** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The cub touches and melds with its bonded vessel, bringing the statue to life. It can cease Inhabiting its Vessel by [Dismissing](/Rules.aspx?ID=2245) the effect. While Inhabiting the Vessel, it loses its [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) and gains: Immunities [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661); Resistances physical 3 (except bludgeoning); Speed 20 feet; and the following Strike.
  • Melee jaws +10 ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), Damage 1d6+5 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)
**Spirit Body** When not Inhabiting its Vessel, the cub is [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) and gains resistance 3 to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) damage and damage from Strikes with the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune; double resistance against non-magical).
Bleeding Omen Legends say that stone lions can predict incoming storms or floods. If a stone lion's eyes, nose, or ears bleed, preparations should be made. Lone survivors of these disasters claim those who disparage such omens as superstition often don't live to see the next dawn.
name: Stone Lion Cub

markdown:
Stone lions are a common sight outside of banks, temples, palaces, and even private residences. The fierce expressions affixed on their faces remind passersby to stay on their best behavior. Stone lions remain faithful guardians of a site, regardless of whether it falls into disrepair. Even if people abandon a place, the lion remains dutiful until its statue is too worn or broken to inhabit any longer. Stone lions are carved in complementary pairs in order for each to defend their wards from different types of threat. Traditionally, one lion defends from spiritual threats, while the other defends against physical dangers. Occasionally, they subvert this trend; one notable manor in Quain is guarded by two proud, identical male lions. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Stone Lion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4430) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 183 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 60 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +8, [Meteorology Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +11, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +13 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 **Anchored Soul** The stone lion is mystically bonded to their bonded vessel and must remain within 1 mile of it. Some might be further restricted to the location they guard. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +9 **Will** +13 +1 status to all saves vs. [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) **HP** 50 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) **Bonded Vessel** The condition of a stone lion's vessel dictates the lion's maximum Hit Point value. Undamaged, the vessel is an object with 50 hit Points (BT 25). When the cub is in spirit form, damaging it doesn't hurt the vessel, but damaging the vessel deals an equal amount of damage to the lion. When the lion Inhabits its Vessel, they're a single target, and damage reduces the Hit Points of both the lion and the vessel. If the vessel is broken, the lion can still fight normally while inhabiting it and takes no ill effect, but if the vessel is ever destroyed, the lion is instantly slain and can't reconstitute.
**Reconstitution** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When the lion reaches 0 Hit Points, its spirit dissipates. If its bonded vessel is intact, the lion re-forms in this vessel after 2d4 days, fully healed. If the vessel is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), it must first be Repaired, after which the cub reforms in 3d4 days. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Inhabit Vessel** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The lion touches and melds with its bonded vessel, bringing the statue to life. It can cease Inhabiting its Vessel by [Dismissing](/Rules.aspx?ID=2245) the effect. While Inhabiting the Vessel, it loses its [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) and gains: Immunities [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661); but Resistances physical 5 (except bludgeoning), Speed 20 feet, and it gains the following Strikes.
  • **Melee** jaws +14, **Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)
  • **Ranged** stone ball +13 (range increment 30 feet), **Damage** 2d4+7 bludgeoning
**Spirit Body** When not Inhabiting its Vessel, the cub is [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) and gains resistance 5 to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) damage and damage from Strikes with the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune; double resistance against non-magical).
name: Stone Lion

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Gug](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4431)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A gug's most horrid feature is its barrel-shaped head, which splits vertically to reveal numerous rows of sharp, yellow teeth and an open throat. Its eyes, located on either side of its head-jaw, are small but keen. Bony ridges protect a gug's eyes from the frantic flailing of its prey, as it prefers meals of raw and writhing meat over fungi and molds. It grips said prey with powerful arms that split at the elbow into a pair of forearms, giving it four clawed paws. These monstrous brutes are covered with shaggy black fur, often crusted with blood and gore. 

Although gugs may seem bestial, they have keen and wicked intellects. Gugs lair far underground, but they sometimes come to the surface to hunt on dark nights, either alone or in small groups. As they possess voracious appetites, most gugs consume the creatures they catch, but some instead kidnap their victims and retreat below the surface, leaving only a lingering stench and odd, clawed paw prints. Victims are taken to rancid lairs marked with strange runes and sacrificed to the gugs' wicked gods of blood, darkness, and nightmares. Dire rumors tell of lightless gug cities made of titanic blocks of stone far underground, where powerful gug leaders preach their vile doctrines to howling mobs of their fellows.

Gugs have a strange relationship with ghouls, which seems to date from their shared origin in a distant subterranean world. Gugs live in fear of ghouls, despite towering over them. Some ghouls use this to their advantage by influencing gugs to attack opposed underground communities.

Gugs stand 16 feet tall and weigh 2,000 pounds, although they have an eerie, graceful gait that belies their immense size. Their light step and ability to squeeze through very small crannies makes gugs common bogeymen in tales of strange disappearances or bloody massacres.

Some particularly bloodthirsty gugs gain awful powers as gifts from their eldritch patrons. These monsters, known as savants, are never less than 12th level in power and gain several [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) [innate spells](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901). Though each savant's precise mix of spells varies, they normally grant [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), offer power to manipulate and change rock, or invoke awful and destructive energies upon living flesh.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 27

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

</column>

---

**Speed** 40 feet, climb 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +23 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d12+13 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d8+13 slashing

**Eerie Flexibility**   Despite its size, the gug's multiple joints allow it to fit through tight spaces as if it were a Medium creature. While [Squeezing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372), it can move at its full Speed.

**Furious Claws** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The gug makes up to four claw Strikes, each against a different target. These attacks all count toward the gug's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after the gug makes all its attacks.

**[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73)** <actions string="Single Action" />  claw

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Eldritch Gods</title>

Gugs don't traditionally make religion and faith a key part of their society, but gug cities always feature prominent temples to obscure gods whose faith has passed from view on most mortal worlds. Most gugs certainly know of entities such as [Azathoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=567), [Nyarlathotep](/Deities.aspx?ID=573), and [Yog-Sothoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=580), but even among gugs, these [Outer Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13) are more feared and respected than prayed to.
</aside>
name: Gug

markdown:
The bizarre hadrinnexes resemble defensive systems more than living creatures. They evolve at a rapid rate but only to specific attacks against them, which suggests they were created through advanced technology or magic. The husk surrounding a hadrinnex—flecks of metal suspended in a malleable organic carapace—reshapes in response to harm. So, too, can a glowing gland within the creature's thorax, which collects energy and restructures the creature's biology to protect it from that energy. This organ is fragile and ruptures soon after a hadrinnex is killed. Hadrinnexes have only a rudimentary intellect and usually follow more intelligent aberrations. As bodyguards or peons, they perform simple tasks diligently. Though poor at problem-solving and improvisation, hadrinnexes' dependability and adaptive physiology make them ideal for dangerous tasks in hazardous environments. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Hadrinnex](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4432) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 185 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) ([touch](/Rules.aspx?ID=2238)) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +11 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -3 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +13 **Will** +17 **HP** 120 **Resistances** energy 15, physical 15 (see Rapid Evolution) **Rapid Evolution** ([morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=657), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** The hadrinnex takes damage of a physical or energy damage type (bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing for physical; [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) for energy); **Effect** The hadrinnex reconfigures its husk (if triggered by physical damage) or its energy gland (if triggered by energy damage). Any reconfiguration applies to the triggering damage and lasts until the next time the hadrinnex uses Rapid Evolution.
  • **Energy Gland** Reconfiguring the energy gland changes both the hadrinnex's energy damage resistance and the damage of its energy ray to the triggering energy damage type. By default, the energy gland is configured to sonic.
  • **Husk** The hadrinnex's physical damage resistance and the damage of its weapon arm Strikes change to the triggering type. Weapon arm Strikes gain an additional trait depending on the current damage type: bludgeoning adds [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694), piercing adds [deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), and slashing adds [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708). By default, the husk is configured to bludgeoning.
 --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** weapon arm +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+9 physical (see Rapid Evolution) **Ranged** energy ray +18 ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 5d6 energy (see Rapid Evolution) **Extend Limbs** The hadrinnex makes two weapon arm Strikes, each targeting a different creature. The hadrinnex's reach increases to 20 feet for these Strikes. **Vent Energy** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The hadrinnex purges the energy in its energy gland for an external discharge. It either blasts the energy to deal 7d6 energy damage to creatures in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) or directs the energy to its weapon arms, making its weapon arm Strikes deal an extra 2d6 energy damage for 1 minute. Either one expends the damage type stored in the hadrinnex's energy gland, which then goes dormant. The hadrinnex loses its energy resistance and can't use energy ray until it uses Rapid Evolution to reconfigure its energy gland again. Directing energy to its weapon arms again removes any previous energy boost to its weapon arm.
Hibernation Pods Hadrinnexes can be found hibernating in remote areas, their husks shaped into ovoid pods. These pods are usually centered in craters, suggesting the hadrinnex fell from above. Leaving hibernation takes 2 months as it extrudes its limbs and restarts biological processes. This can be accelerated with a daily influx of energy to its energy gland.
name: Hadrinnex

markdown:
Ruthless and temperamental, storm hags are the personification of a storm's rage. Outcasts by their own behavior, storm hags are often found on islands they've made into personal havens, controlling the local inhabitants through threats. Unlike most hags, many storm hags show signs of genuinely caring for their offspring, both those adopted and by birth. Any such children who possess morals aren't in for an easy life, however, as they're usually left trying and failing to deal with their mother's implacable temper toward others. A storm hag's true form resembles an older humanoid woman with stormtossed white hair, lightning-bright eyes, and dark blue skin. They typically only show their true form when in a rage, which makes their natural visage seem more terrifying than it actually is. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Storm Hag](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4433) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 186 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), stormsight **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111); voice of the storm **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +13, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +11, [Weather Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Coven** A storm hag adds [_hydraulic torrent_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562), [_lightning storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2007), and [_mariner's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1593) to their coven's spells.
**Stormsight** Wind, precipitation, and clouds don't impair a storm hag's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from storms, mist, precipitation, and the like.
**Voice of the Storm** A storm hag can send spoken messages or sounds on the wind to any spot that they've seen and the wind can reach within a 50-mile radius. They decide whether it's clearly audible or barely heard above the wind. The message is delivered regardless of whether anyone is present to hear it. The hag can use this ability to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) creatures that hear their message with dire threats or unnerving whispers of doom. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +9 **Will** +12 **HP** 90 **Immunities** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), wind mastery **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 5 **Wind Mastery** A storm hag is unaffected by strong winds, natural or magical. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet; storm passage **Melee** claw +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d4+4 slashing plus 1d12 electricity **Ranged** cutting gale +13 ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d6 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 22 - **3rd** [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550) (at will), [Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586), [Summon Elemental](/Spells.aspx?ID=1698) (air or water only), [Wall of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1753) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The storm hag can take on the appearance of any Medium humanoid woman. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes, but it might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Storm Passage** The storm hag ignores difficult terrain caused by wind, rain, and other stormy weather. **Stormcalling** ([Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=580)) A storm hag can perform a special control weather ritual, which requires no secondary casters, to change the weather within 5 miles of their location for 4d12 hours. The primary check is a DC 23 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) check, and they can't get an outcome worse than a failure. The storm hag can create only hurricanes, thunderstorms, and tornadoes, but they can do so regardless of the current season. They can also quell natural weather events but almost never willingly do so.
name: Storm Hag
